Japanese: 午前１時をお知らせします。
English: The time is 1:00 AM.

Japanese: ピッ‥‥ピッ‥‥ピッ‥‥ ポーン‥‥
English: Beep... Beep... Beep... Beeeeeeep...

Japanese: 刑事さん！ 予告の時間をすぎましたぞ！
English: Detective! We made it!

Japanese: ふう‥‥どうやら、 宝石はブジだったようですな。
English: Whew... What a relief. Glad the jewel is still safe.

Japanese: きっと、ワレワレの警備に恐れを なしたッスね！
English: Ho ho! You said it, pal. It musta been our rock solid security that scared him off!

Japanese: いちおう、金庫の中を カクニンしてもらえますかな？
English: Would you mind opening the safe just to double check?

Japanese: あああああああッ！ やられたッスウゥゥゥッ！
English: Aaaaaaaaaah! We've been had!

Japanese: お‥‥オモテだッ！　警官隊！ 探照灯をつけろおッ！
English: O-Out front! Guards! Turn on the searchlights!

Japanese: わははははははははははははは‥‥ ‥‥ゴクロウだったねえ、諸君！
English: Aha ha ha ha ha ha ha... Better luck next time, gentlemen!

Japanese: わははははははははははははは‥‥ それでは、諸君。シツレイするよ！
English: Aha ha ha ha ha ha ha... Excuse me, but I'm afraid I must be leaving now!

Japanese: 次の満月の夜‥‥また会おう！ わははははははははははははは‥‥
English: We shall meet again... When the next moon is full! Aha ha ha ha ha ha ha...

Japanese: わーははははははははははは‥‥
English: Ahaaaa ha ha ha ha ha ha...

Japanese: ９月１１日　午後　３時２４分 成歩堂法律事務所
English: October 11, 3:24 PM Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: ね、ね、なるほどくん！ ちょっと聞いてよ！
English: Hey, Nick! Get a load of this!

Japanese: ほらほら、トイレ掃除なんて あと、あと！
English: Hey, are you listening to me!? You can clean the toilet later! This is important!

Japanese: ‥‥うるさいな。 何をハシャいでるんだよ。
English: *sigh* What are you freaking out about now?

Japanese: ふふん。
English: Hee hee.

Japanese: このあたしに向かって、エラそうな 口をきけるのも、今日かぎりだよ！
English: Today will be the last time you talk to me that way!
Huh?

Japanese: あたしたち、 ついにビッグになれるかも！
English: We're about to hit the big time!

Japanese: “ビッグ”‥‥？ 真宵ちゃんと、ぼくが？
English: "Big time"? And what do you mean by "we"? You don't mean you and me are...?

Japanese: ナニ言ってるの、なるほどくん。
English: Ha! Don't be silly.

Japanese: あたしと‥‥ はみちゃんに決まってるでしょ！
English: I'm talking about me and Pearly, of course!

Japanese: こんにちは。 おひさしぶりです、なるほどくん。
English: Hello. It's a pleasure to see you again, Mr. Nick!

Japanese: あ、春美ちゃん。よく来たね。 ‥‥何かあったの？
English: Pearls! You haven't changed a bit! Wait... What are you doing here anyway?

Japanese: ふふ。なるほどくん、 何も知らないのですね。
English: Hee hee. Haven't you heard, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: ほら、これですっ！
English: Here! Take a look at this!

Japanese: （なんだ、これ‥‥。ポスター？）
English: (What's this...? Some kind of poster?)

Japanese: 倉院の里（くらいんのさと） ‥‥って、あの？
English: Kurain Village... Isn't that...?

Japanese: そうだよ。 あたしと、はみちゃんのふるさと。
English: That's right. It's our hometown... Pearly and mine, that is.

Japanese: “秘宝”‥‥なんて、あったっけ？ あんなイナカに。
English: What's this about "treasures" from the boonies?

Japanese: ふっふっふっ‥‥ あたしたちをナメちゃダメだよ。
English: Ha ha ha. Very funny. You can laugh all you want...

Japanese: 今夜、ゆっくり見せてあげるから。
English: But you'll be singing another tune tonight!

Japanese: こ、こんや‥‥？
English: Tonight? What about tonight?

Japanese: 《倉院の里・ひほうてん》は、 来週から始まるのですが‥‥
English: The "Treasures of Kurain" exhibit doesn't actually start until next week, but...

Japanese: 主催者のかたが、トクベツに 招待してくださったのです！
English: ...the promoter sent us some special VIP entry passes!

Japanese: だからね。ちょっと今日は オシャレしてきちゃった！　どう？
English: That's why I dressed up extra special today! What do you think, Nick?

Japanese: （いつもと変わらない‥‥）
English: (Heh. Same Maya. Different Day.)

Japanese: この子は、綾里　真宵 （あやさとまよい）ちゃん。
English: This young lady here is Maya Fey...

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくの師匠、 綾里　千尋さんのイモウトさんだ。
English: The younger sister of Mia Fey... ...My friend and mentor.

Japanese: 彼女と知り合ったのは‥‥ 今から、２年前。
English: I first met her two years ago...

Japanese: ‥‥千尋さんが亡くなった、 ある事件がキッカケだった‥‥。
English: I was working on the case surrounding Mia's death, and ever since then...

Japanese: 今は、この法律事務所の “影の所長”やってます！
English: I've been the one who's been keeping this law office afloat from behind the scenes!

Japanese: ‥‥というのは、 世をしのぶ仮の姿で‥‥、
English: Actually that's just a cover for her true identity...

Japanese: じつは、一人前の霊媒師をめざして 修行中の、とても怪しげな女の子。
English: In reality, she's a spirit medium, and a bit of a shady character.

Japanese: ちょっと！ ナニよ“怪しげ”って！
English: Hey! Who are you calling "shady"!?

Japanese: そして、この子は綾里　春美 （あやさとはるみ）ちゃん。
English: And this little girl is Pearl Fey, but I usually call her "Pearls".

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんのイトコで、 これまた霊媒師のタマゴだ。
English: She's Maya's cousin and a spirit medium in training herself.

Japanese: ふつつかものですが、 よろしくおねがいいたします！
English: I know I'm a bit young, but I want to help in any way I can, Mr. Nick!

Japanese: ２人のふるさと・倉院の里は、 倉院流霊媒道の総本山。
English: Kurain Village is the home of the mysterious Kurain Channeling Technique.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんは、その倉院流霊媒道の 家元のムスメにあたるという。
English: And Maya here is the daughter of the Kurain School of Channeling's Master.

Japanese: ‥‥冗談みたいなハナシだが、 決してそうじゃない。
English: ...I know it sounds ridiculous, but it's no joke.

Japanese: ぼくはもう、何度もその トンでもない霊媒のチカラを‥‥
English: I've seen her power with my own eyes... It's the real thing alright.

Japanese: ホラホラ、なるほどくん。 いいから、さっさと用意する！
English: Earth to Nick! How long are you going to make two gorgeous women like us wait!?

Japanese: もうわたくし、待ちきれません。 ひほうてん‥‥楽しみですっ！
English: Yes, Mr. Nick! I can't wait any longer! I want to see the exhibit!

Japanese: （やれやれ。つきあうしか ないみたいだな‥‥）
English: (*sigh* Looks like I don't get a choice here. Might as well head on out...)

Japanese: 《秘宝展のポスター》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Treasure Exhibit Poster added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ９月１２日　午前１０時　９分 成歩堂法律事務所
English: October 12, 10:09 AM Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: な、な、なるほどくん！ タイヘンだよっ！
English: N-Nick! This is terrible!

Japanese: ‥‥もう！　また トイレ掃除なんかやってるし！
English: ...What the--!? You're cleaning the toilet again!?

Japanese: そんなにトイレ好きだとは 思わなかったよっ！
English: I never knew you were such a toilet freak!

Japanese: なんだよ朝っぱらから‥‥
English: Gimme a break, would you? My brain just hit the snooze button for the fifth time...

Japanese: あの。わたくし、 テレビつけますから！
English: Um... I'm going to turn on the TV!

Japanese: ‥‥では、先ほどお伝えした 盗難事件の続報です！
English: ...We've got an update on the recent treasure heist!

Japanese: 警察当局は、犯行の手口などから、 今回の事件の犯人を‥‥
English: Based on clues found at the scene of the crime, authorities have announced...

Japanese: あの《怪人☆仮面マスク》であると 正式に発表しました！
English: ...that they believe it was work of the renowned phantom thief, Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: ‥‥か、“かめんますく”‥‥？
English: ...M-Masque the Mask...?

Japanese: 高菱屋百貨店には、数日前、 予告状が届いていたらしく‥‥、
English: According to a spokesperson, Lordly Tailor received a threat letter some days ago.

Japanese: 秘宝ばかりを狙う怪人の犯行は、 これで５件目になります！
English: This is the fifth heist by this phantom thief, who only targets rare treasures.

Japanese: ‥‥た、“高菱屋百貨店”‥‥？
English: L-Lordly Tailor...!?

Japanese: どーよ、なるほどくん！
English: Well, Nick!? What are you going to do!?

Japanese: これでもまだ、 トイレ掃除をしようっての！
English: Don't you dare go back to scrubbing the toilet!

Japanese: “秘宝が盗まれた”って ‥‥まさか？
English: Treasure...? Lordly Tailor...? You don't think...!?

Japanese: やられちゃったんだよ！ 仮面マスクに！
English: Yes! Now get up, Nick! It was stolen! By Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: あたしたちの秘宝が‥‥
English: Our most valuable treasure...

Japanese: あの《倉院のツボ》が、 盗まれちゃったんだよおお！
English: The Kurain Sacred Urn was STOLEN!!

Japanese: えええええええええッ！
English: Whaaat!?

Japanese: その、怪人に盗まれた 《倉院のツボ》ってのは‥‥
English: Refresh my memory a little... What's this "Sacred Urn"...?

Japanese: 倉院の里で、イチバン大事な お宝なんだよ！
English: It's only the most important treasure in Kurain Village! That's all!

Japanese: ほら！　このポスターにも デカデカと！
English: Look! It's right there on this poster!

Japanese: このツボにはね。タマシイが 封じこめられているんだよ！
English: Don't you remember what's inside? The urn contains a very important soul!

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道の創始者、 あの綾里　供子さまのタマシイが！
English: Namely, the soul of Mystic Ami Fey, the founder of the Kurain Channeling Technique!

Japanese: ‥‥ねっ、はみちゃん！
English: Right, Pearly?

Japanese: え！　ええ。そ、そうですともっ！
English: Ah, yes! Th-That's right!
Hold up...

Japanese: このツボ‥‥。たしか、供子さまの お名前が書いてあったのに。
English: I thought that urn had the name "AMI", written on it...

Japanese: いつのまにか《子供》になってる。 ‥‥はみちゃん、知ってた？
English: Now it says "I AM"... Hmm... Any idea how that happened, Pearly?

Japanese: え！　い、いいえ‥‥その。 わたくしは、あの。
English: Huh!? Um, I... W-Well...

Japanese: ふふ、フシギなコトも あるものなのですね‥‥
English: Tee hee... There are some things best left unsolved, wouldn't you say, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: （１年前‥‥真宵ちゃんのふるさと 倉院の里で殺人事件が起こった）
English: (That's right! One year ago, there was a murder in Kurain Village, Maya's hometown.)

Japanese: （倉院のツボは‥‥その事件で 重要な手がかりになったんだ）
English: (The Sacred Urn... It turned out to be an important clue in that case.)

Japanese: 《倉院のツボ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Sacred Urn added to the Court Record.

Japanese: さて。これからどうしようか。 トイレ掃除が終わったら、次は‥‥
English: OK, the toilet is shinier than the judge's head, so let's see what's next...

Japanese: ナニ言ってんの、なるほどくん！
English: What's wrong with you, Nick!?

Japanese: な、なんだよ。
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: “なんだよ”じゃないで
English: What do you mean what do I--

Japanese: なるほどくんっ！
English: Mr. Nick!

Japanese: イトしい真宵さまの秘宝が 盗まれたのですよ！
English: Your beloved Mystic Maya's treasure has been stolen!

Japanese: なんとも思わないのですかッ！
English: Doesn't that even bother you!?

Japanese: い、いや、盗まれたツボは 倉院の里の秘宝であって‥‥
English: But I thought the urn was the village's treasure, so I don't see how...

Japanese: 同じコトですッ！ 真宵さまは、家元をつぐお方。
English: The village IS Mystic Maya! She's the future Master of the Kurain School of Channeling!

Japanese: 知らないとは言わせません！ ‥‥ええ、言わせませんとも！
English: You know what I'm talking about, Mr. Nick! I won't let you say you don't!

Japanese: そ、それはいいけど‥‥ ぼくにどうしろと？
English: O-OK OK! I do! ...So what am I supposed to do about it?

Japanese: 決まってるではありませんか！ ワルモノをひっとらえるのです！
English: Isn't it obvious? Go and find the bad guy who did this!

Japanese: そうだそうだ！ 倉院のツボ、取り戻してよー！
English: Yeah! And get the Sacred Urn back!

Japanese: で、でも。 ぼくは探偵じゃなくて弁護士な
English: B-But... I'm not a detective... I'm a lawyer.

Japanese: いーえ、カンケイありません！
English: That has nothing to do with this!

Japanese: 殿方ならば！　イトしいヒトの ために、今こそ立ち上がるのです！
English: If you're a real gentleman, you would find it for your beloved Mystic Maya!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥春美ちゃんの 思いこみには負けるなあ‥‥）
English: (Man, oh man... There's no winning against Pearls's fairy-tale image of love...)

Japanese: で‥‥なんて言ったっけ？ そのワルモノの名前。
English: ...So what's the name of this bad guy again?

Japanese: 怪人☆仮面マスクだよ、 なるほどくん！
English: Mask☆DeMasque, Nick! Make a note of it! Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: いやー、タイヘンだったよねえ。 あたしがタイホされちゃってさ。
English: Ah, it was terrible! I was arrested and everything!

Japanese: １年前‥‥倉院の里で 起こった殺人事件。
English: One year ago... The murder in Kurain Village.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんの霊媒儀式の最中、 被害者は殺害された。
English: A man was killed during one of Maya's spirit channelings.

Japanese: このツボは、彼女の無実を 立証する証拠になったんだ‥‥
English: And this urn wound up being the key piece of evidence that proved her innocence.

Japanese: あの‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Umm... Mr. Nick?

Japanese: 真宵さまには‥‥ナイショに してもらえますか？
English: Would you mind not telling Mystic Maya about that?

Japanese: あのとき、わたくしが、その。 ツボを割ってしまったコト‥‥
English: You promised, remember? You said you wouldn't tell anyone I broke the urn...

Japanese: ああ、そうだったっけね。
English: Oh yeah, that's right.

Japanese: （このツボ‥‥たしかに最初は 《供子》と書いてあったらしい）
English: (Now I remember. This urn used to say "AMI" on it...)

Japanese: （‥‥それを、うっかり 割っちゃったのが、春美ちゃん）
English: (Until Pearls accidentally broke it into a million pieces.)

Japanese: （コッソリ修理したんだけど‥‥ うまく行かなかったそうだ）
English: (She tried to secretly fix it... Let's just say it didn't work out so well.)

Japanese: わたくし‥‥工作がニガテで、 漢字も知らないものですから‥‥
English: Sorry... I'm not so good at arts and crafts... Spelling either...

Japanese: ‥‥で、その結果できあがったのが この、ユガんだツボ。
English: ...Anyway, this is how her gluing project turned out.

Japanese: 破片をムリヤリくっつけたから、 《供子》が《子供》になったとか。
English: She put the pieces together wrong and now, instead of "AMI", it says, "I AM".

Japanese: ‥‥よく、今まで１年間も バレなかったよなあ。
English: ...I can't believe no one's noticed this until now, a whole year later.

Japanese: わたくしも、そう思います‥‥。
English: I can't believe it myself...

Japanese: 観葉植物のチャーリーくん。
English: Charley. A quite decorative plant.

Japanese: 生前、千尋さんがかわいがっていた 事務所のマスコット。
English: When she was alive, Mia really loved it. She even went so far as to make it our mascot.

Japanese: ‥‥いいよなあ。おまえは いつも、のんびりしてて。
English: It must be nice to be a plant, being able to just sit in the sun and photosynthesize...

Japanese: コラ！　なるほどくん！ しみじみしない！
English: Hey! Nick! No time for daydreaming!

Japanese: ダメですよ！ しみじみしていては。
English: That's right, Mr. Nick! No time for daydreaming!

Japanese: （秘宝が出てくるまで、 平和はないな、こりゃ‥‥）
English: (Oh man. Until I find that urn, they're not gonna give me a second of peace...)

Japanese: ムズカシイ法律の本が あざ笑うように並んでいる。
English: Difficult-looking legal books stand in a formidable row. They mock me.

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、うっすらと ホコリをかぶってるな。
English: ...Actually, I've neglected them for so long that they're covered in a layer of dust.

Japanese: たまにはちょっと 読んでみるか。
English: Maybe I should actually try reading them once in a while.

Japanese: コラ！　なるほどくん！ よりによって今、読まない！
English: Hey, Nick! This is no time for reading!

Japanese: あとにしましょう！ なるほどくん！
English: Yes, Mr. Nick! You can always read later!

Japanese: （秘宝が出てくるまで、 平和はないな、こりゃ‥‥）
English: (I guess I won't be getting any peace and quiet to even read until I find that urn...)

Japanese: ぼくのデスクだ。
English: It's my desk.

Japanese: あまり座る機会がないため、 ちょっとホコリっぽいな。
English: I don't get to use it much, so the dust bunnies are starting to mount their assault.

Japanese: ‥‥たまには、そうじでも してみるか。
English: Maybe I should wipe them out with a few well-placed swipes of my cleaning rag...

Japanese: コラ！　なるほどくん！ ワザとやってるでしょ！
English: Hey! Nick! Are you trying to make me upset!?

Japanese: おそうじなら、 わたくしがやりますっ！
English: Yeah, Mr. Nick! If you're worried about the cleaning, please leave it up to me!

Japanese: （秘宝が出てくるまで、 平和はないな、こりゃ‥‥）
English: (At least Pearls is good at cleaning. *sigh* Guess I'd better go find that urn...)

Japanese: そんなに値打ちモノなの？ このボロっちいツボが。
English: Is this thing really all that valuable? It looks kinda... well... junky, if you ask me.

Japanese: だって、タマシイ入りだよ！ 綾里　供子さまの！
English: But it's got the soul of Mystic Ami, the founder of our channeling technique!

Japanese: 売ったら、いくらぐらいに なるんだろう？
English: So how much do you think you could get for it?

Japanese: “売る”‥‥？ その発想はなかったなあ‥‥。
English: What!? Sell the Sacred Urn!? You know, I never thought about that...

Japanese: ちょっと、はみちゃん。 メモしといて。
English: Hey Pearly... Make a note of that.

Japanese: “お金のたまる方法、その１０９。 お宝を売りはらうべし”
English: "Ways to Save Money, Tip 109: Sell treasures whenever possible."

Japanese: ‥‥うりはらうべし、と。
English: ...Whenever possible, check!

Japanese: （こんなツボ、買う人なんか いないと思うけどな‥‥）
English: (Somehow, I think Maya's going to be searching for a buyer for a long time...)

Japanese: ‥‥どうかな？　これなんだけど。
English: So, what do you think about this?

Japanese: どうかな？　はみちゃん。
English: Hmm... What about it, Pearly?

Japanese: いかがなものでしょう？ なるほどくん。
English: I'm not certain. What do you think, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: （特に手がかりはなし、と）
English: (Maybe three heads aren't better than one...)

Japanese: かわいいよねー、はみちゃん。
English: Pearly is such a cutie, isn't she Nick?

Japanese: そうだなあ。
English: Yup.

Japanese: あたしに似て、 おリコウさんだしね。
English: And she's smart too, just like yours truly!

Japanese: “スエはハカセかダイジンか” ‥‥なーんて。
English: She's got quite a future ahead of her! I bet she could be President some day!

Japanese: （霊媒師じゃないのか‥‥？）
English: (A President that can channel past Presidents... Hmm... That just might come in handy...)

Japanese: あ、お姉ちゃん。 また、会いたいな‥‥。
English: S-Sis... *sniffle* I really miss her...

Japanese: 千尋さま‥‥エラい弁護士さん だったのですよね？
English: Was Mystic Mia... Was she a really good lawyer?

Japanese: そうだよ。 なるほどくんの、お師匠さん。
English: Yeah, she was awesome! She taught Nick everything he knows.

Japanese: なるほどくん。千尋さまに 負けないよう、修行するのですよ。
English: You should train hard and try to make her proud, Mr. Nick!

Japanese: う、うん。がんばるよ。
English: Y-Yeah, I'll do my best.

Japanese: 今度、わたくしといっしょに 滝に打たれましょうよ！
English: You can join me on my next trip to a freezing waterfall!

Japanese: （霊媒師の修行と いっしょにされてしまった）
English: (I hate to break it to her, but I don't think being frozen to the bone helps in court...)

Japanese: 写真うつり、よくないね。
English: You're not very photogenic, are you?

Japanese: 会話が終わってしまいましたね！
English: I guess that's the end of that conversation!

Japanese: ‥‥どうかな？ このヒトなんだけど。
English: So, what do you think about this person?

Japanese: どうかな？　はみちゃん。
English: Hmm... What do you think, Pearly?

Japanese: いかがなものでしょう？ なるほどくん。
English: I'm not certain. What do you think, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: （特に手がかりはなし、と）
English: (I think the saying I'm looking for is, "never seen them before in my life"...)

Japanese: お。あたしのあげた勾玉だ。 どう？　役に立ってるでしょ？
English: Oh, hey! It's my Magatama! So what do you think, Nick? Isn't it neat?

Japanese: そうだね。なにしろ、人の ココロのヒミツが見えるからね。
English: It sure is. Being able to peek into people's minds is definitely a nice advantage.

Japanese: ええッ！　そんなコトが できるんだ！
English: What? How do you do that!?

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃんがくれたんだぞ、 これ‥‥。
English: What do you mean? You gave me this thing...

Japanese: ま、そうだけど。勾玉に霊力を こめたのは、はみちゃんだからね。
English: Well, I knew Pearly put some sort of spiritual power into it...

Japanese: あたし、それでナニができるか よく知らないんだよね。
English: But I had no idea that it let you do something as cool as that!

Japanese: ‥‥たよりない家元だな‥‥。
English: ...Some future leader you are.

Japanese: 今度、あたしにも見せてね。 ココロのヒミツ。
English: Next time you use it, let me know, OK!? I want to see into people's minds, too!

Japanese: まだ持ってたんだ、そのバッジ。
English: So you've still got that badge, I see.

Japanese: そ、そりゃそうだろう。 弁護士なんだから。
English: Huh!? Well, I'm a lawyer, aren't I?

Japanese: いやー、こんなに長く つづくとは思わなかったよ。
English: Yeah, but I guess I just didn't think you'd keep on being one for this long.

Japanese: あぶなっかしいからねー、 なるほどくんのベンゴ。
English: You have to admit, you've had some close brushes with death because of your job.

Japanese: （それは、真宵ちゃんの霊媒も 似たようなモノだと思うけど）
English: (...I fail to see how being a lawyer is more dangerous than channeling dead people.)

Japanese: あたしも知らなかったよ、 こんなものがあったなんて。
English: You know, even I didn't know this statue existed until a little while ago.

Japanese: “倉院流・分家の霊場から 借りてきた”って言ってたな。
English: Didn't Adrian say it's from a Fey branch family's training hall or something?

Japanese: 全国に、たくさんあるんだよ。 倉院流霊媒道の修行場。
English: Yes, there are actually a lot of training halls scattered all over the country.

Japanese: さみしい山奥に、ひっそりと。 いくつあるか知らないけど。
English: Hidden away deep in desolate mountains...

Japanese: （‥‥さみしくなくちゃ ダメなのかな、修行場‥‥）
English: (Why does it always have to be "desolate" mountains...?)

Japanese: 観葉植物のチャーリーくん。
English: Charley. A quite decorative plant.

Japanese: 生前、千尋さんがかわいがっていた 事務所のマスコット。
English: When she was alive, Mia really loved it. She even went so far as to make it our mascot.

Japanese: どんなにいそがしくても、 こいつの世話だけは忘れない。
English: No matter how busy I get, I never forget to water this little fella.

Japanese: 窓の向かいがわには、 巨大なビルがそびえている。
English: There is a giant building just outside the window.

Japanese: 《ホテル・バンドー》 超ゴウカな超一流ホテルだ。
English: It's the Gatewater Hotel, a high-class, luxury hotel.

Japanese: なんでも、となり街の一角を ゴッソリ買収して、
English: The chain is getting so rich that they bought a whole chunk of the next town over...

Japanese: 超レジャー施設《バンドーランド》 の建設が始まったらしい。
English: ...and started building a huge theme park. It's going to be called "Gatewater Land".

Japanese: ‥‥２年前は小さな ビジネスホテルだったのに。
English: ...And to think that two years ago, it was just a little hotel for businessmen.

Japanese: ムズカシイ法律の本が あざ笑うように並んでいる。
English: Difficult-looking legal books stand in a formidable row. They mock me.

Japanese: ぜんぶ、千尋さんが 残していったモノだ。
English: These were all Mia's...

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、自分で買った本、 ひとつもないな。
English: Wow, I just realized I didn't buy a single one of these.

Japanese: ぼくのデスクだ。
English: It's my desk.

Japanese: あまり座る機会がないため、 いつもはホコリっぽいんだけど‥‥
English: I don't get to use it much, so the dust bunnies are starting to mount their assault.

Japanese: 今日は、まぶしいほどピカピカだ。 ‥‥きっと、春美ちゃんだな。
English: Hm? Today it's sparkly clean. I guess Pearls must've wiped them out for me.

Japanese: 古い映画のポスターだ。
English: An old movie poster.

Japanese: ムカシ千尋さんが見て、 初めて泣いた映画なのだそうだ。
English: Apparently, this was the first movie that made Mia cry when she saw it, a long time ago.

Japanese: 最近、妹の真宵ちゃんが見て、 一晩中泣いていたらしい。
English: Maya watched it recently, and said she cried all night, too!

Japanese: ‥‥ということで、最近は このポスターを張ってある。
English: ...Which, I guess, is why it's back up on the wall.

Japanese: 今度、ぼくも見てみるか。
English: I'll have to check it out one of these days.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 成歩堂法律事務所
English: October 12 Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: あ‥‥ お、おかえりなさいませ！
English: Ah... Mr. Nick! W-Welcome back!

Japanese: ああ、春美ちゃん。 帰っていたんだ。
English: Oh, hey Pearls. You're back too, I see.

Japanese: わ、わたくし、おだんごを 買っておきましたから！
English: I, um... I went and bought us a strawberry cake!

Japanese: 今、お茶をいれますねっ！
English: I'll go make some tea to go with it!

Japanese: あ、春美ちゃん‥‥
English: Hey, uh Pearls...

Japanese: 気にしてるみたいだね、 留置所のコト‥‥
English: It looks like she feels really bad about what happened at the detention center...

Japanese: な‥‥なるほどくん！ わたくし、見そこないましたっ！
English: M-Mr. Nick...! I-I was wrong about you... I shouldn't have trusted you!

Japanese: わたくし‥‥わたくし‥‥ ゼッタイ、ユルせませんっ！
English: I-I... I'll never forgive you... EVER!

Japanese: さあ。お茶がはいりましたよ、 真宵さま！
English: Mystic Maya! The tea is ready!

Japanese: あ‥‥どうもありがと‥‥
English: Oh... Thanks...

Japanese: ささ。なるほどくん。 おだんごをめしあがれ！
English: Come on, Mr. Nick! Please have some of this cake!

Japanese: うん‥‥ありがとう。 あの、春美ちゃん‥‥？
English: Yeah... Thanks. Umm, Pearls?

Japanese: あ！　わたくし、おトイレ掃除の 途中ですので‥‥！
English: Oh! Ex-Excuse me... I was in the middle of cleaning the toilet...!

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待って。 トイレなら、ぼくが今朝‥‥
English: Hey, it's OK! I just cleaned it this morning...

Japanese: 行っちゃった‥‥
English: Too late...

Japanese: （‥‥いい機会かもしれない。 彼女のこと、聞いてみるか‥‥）
English: (...This might be a good time for me to ask about her.)

Japanese: じゃ、なるほどくん！ そろそろ調査に出かけようか。
English: Well, Nick! Why don't we go out and start investigating?

Japanese: 事務所にいても、 手がかりは見つからないからね！
English: We're not going to learn anything just sitting around the office!

Japanese: うん。‥‥でも その前に、春美ちゃんと‥‥
English: Yeah, I know... But... First I want to talk to Pearls...

Japanese: ‥‥あの。
English: ...Umm, Mr. Nick?
Yeah...?

Japanese: わたくし‥‥バカでした。
English: I... I acted like a baby.

Japanese: 春美ちゃん‥‥
English: Pearls...

Japanese: 真宵さまが‥‥こんなに なるほどくんを信じているのに‥‥
English: I doubted you... Even though Mystic Maya trusted you completely...

Japanese: わたくし、まだまだ 修行がたりません。
English: I guess I still have a lot of training to do.

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Mr. Nick!

Japanese: は、はい！
English: Y-Yes?

Japanese: わたくし‥‥わたくし、 かならずや、おワビをいたします！
English: I... From the bottom of my heart, I apologize for what I said!

Japanese: い、いやいや。 とんでもない。ぼくのほうこそ
English: Aww, it's OK! I'm the one that should be apologizing!

Japanese: ちょっと、ひとっ走り 行ってきますね！
English: Well, I'm going out now for a little bit.

Japanese: え！　ど、どこへ‥‥？
English: Huh!? Wh-Where are you going...?

Japanese: わたくし、これでも霊媒師の タマゴのはしくれです！
English: I may be small, but I still have a lot of spirit channeling power!

Japanese: きっと‥‥ささやかな 証拠を、見つけてみせますとも！
English: So I'm going to show you I can be useful too... By finding some evidence!

Japanese: あ。ちょっと‥‥！ （‥‥走っていってしまった‥‥）
English: Hey! W-Wait a...! (Ack! She sure runs fast...)

Japanese: なるほどくん。‥‥しばらく、 好きにさせてあげようよ。
English: Nick... Let's back off and give her some room, OK?

Japanese: ‥‥そうだな。
English: ...Yeah.

Japanese: （春美ちゃんと初めて会ったのは、 今から１年前‥‥）
English: (I first met Pearls a year ago...)

Japanese: （倉院の里で起こった 殺人事件のときだった）
English: (It was when that murder happened at Kurain Village.)

Japanese: （‥‥思えば、初めて会った ときから、彼女は‥‥）
English: (I still remember what she said to me when we first met...)

Japanese: あなたがあの、 なるほどくんなのですね。
English: You... You're Mr. Nick, right?

Japanese: あの。 真宵さまの‥‥大切なヒト。
English: You're... You're Mystic Maya's... "special someone"...

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃん。前から フシギに思ってたんだけど‥‥
English: ...Hey, Maya. I've always thought it was because she was young, but...

Japanese: なあに？
English: But what...?

Japanese: 春美ちゃん‥‥なんか、 ゴカイしてるよね。
English: Pearls... I think she's got the wrong idea.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんと、ぼくのコト。
English: About... you and I.

Japanese: え！ あ、ああ‥‥
English: Huh!? Err, I...

Japanese: ‥‥あのね。ちょっと、 事情があるんだよ。
English: ...Umm... There's something you need to understand...
Wh-What is it?

Japanese: うん‥‥なんて言うか、 倉院の里の風習‥‥みたいな。
English: Umm... It's kind of like a Kurain Village custom... Sort of.

Japanese: 倉院の里って、男の人が ほとんどいないんだ。
English: There are hardly any men in Kurain Village.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥ぼくが行ったときも、 ゼンゼン見かけなかったよ。
English: Now that you mention it, I never actually saw any men there when I visited.

Japanese: なるほどくんには‥‥ムカシ、 話したことがあったよね。
English: I'm pretty sure I told you about it a long time ago...

Japanese: あたしたち綾里家の血には、 強い霊力が宿っている、って。
English: About how spiritual powers run very strong in the Fey family.

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。だから、霊媒師を めざして修行するんでしょ。
English: Yeah, you did. And that's why you're undergoing training to be a spirit medium, right?

Japanese: ただ、霊力を受けつぐことが できるの‥‥女のヒトだけなの。
English: Yeah... The thing is, only women can actually inherit the spiritual power.

Japanese: だからね。里の社会は自然に、 女性中心になっちゃうんだ。
English: That's why the whole culture of Kurain Village kinda revolves around its women.

Japanese: まあ‥‥しかたないだろうね。 特殊な世界だから。
English: Well, that's understandable... Nothing wrong with that.

Japanese: そのせいか‥‥
English: Yeah, but because of that...

Japanese: ケッコンしても、 うまくいかない夫婦が多いんだ。
English: There tends to be a lot of unsuccessful marriages.

Japanese: はあ‥‥
English: Oh?

Japanese: 居場所がなくなっちゃって。 男の人は、みんな里を出ていくの。
English: Well, men start to feel left out after a while, and then they just start leaving.

Japanese: ‥‥女の子が産まれたら、ね。
English: ...Especially if the man has a daughter.

Japanese: もしかして‥‥春美ちゃんの お父さんも‥‥？
English: Are you saying that Pearls's father...?

Japanese: ‥‥いないんだよ。 里を出て行っちゃったみたい。
English: ...He's gone. He left the village when she was very young.

Japanese: そうだったんだ‥‥
English: Boy, that's tough...

Japanese: あの子、里の悲しい夫婦の 姿ばっかり見て育っちゃったから。
English: Yeah, and she grew up seeing nothing but unhappy marriages all around her.

Japanese: だから‥‥そういうことに、 とってもビンカンなの。
English: That's why she's so sensitive to things like that.

Japanese: 春美ちゃん‥‥真宵ちゃんのこと、 ホントに好きみたいだね。
English: Pearls seems to love you a lot.

Japanese: イトコだからね。たったひとりの。 それに‥‥
English: Well, it's because I'm her only cousin. And... well...

Japanese: ホラ。去年の事件で、はみちゃんの おかあさんは‥‥
English: Because of the murder case last year, Pearly's mother is...

Japanese: あ、ああ‥‥。
English: Oh yeah...

Japanese: （そう‥‥春美ちゃんの母親、 キミ子さんは、今‥‥）
English: (I remember now... Pearls's mother, Morgan Fey...)

Japanese: （‥‥刑務所の独房で、 刑の執行を待っている‥‥）
English: (She's serving a prison term in isolation right now...)

Japanese: ‥‥だからね。もう、あたししか いないの。あの子の“家族”‥‥
English: So you see? I'm the only family Pearly has right now...

Japanese: でも‥‥それは、 あたしもおんなじだけどね。
English: But... It's the same for me, too.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん‥‥
English: Maya...

Japanese: ‥‥あたしのおかあさんも‥‥
English: My mother's gone, too...

Japanese: やっぱり、おかあさんは、 まだ‥‥？
English: So your mother's still missing...?

Japanese: うん‥‥。どこにいるか わからないんだ。
English: Yeah... And no one has any idea where she is.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんの母親‥‥ 綾里　舞子さん）
English: (Maya's mother... Misty Fey.)

Japanese: （倉院流霊媒道の “家元”ということだ）
English: (The current Master of the Kurain Channeling Technique.)

Japanese: （今から１７年前、ある事件に 巻きこまれて以来、行方不明）
English: (She disappeared 17 years ago after getting involved in a certain police case.)

Japanese: ‥‥あの。生きているのは たしかなの？
English: ...But you're sure she's still alive, right?

Japanese: うん‥‥わかるんだ。 あたしたち‥‥霊媒師だからね。
English: Yeah... I know she is... It's sort of a spirit medium thing...

Japanese: もし‥‥このまま、おかあさんが あらわれなかったら‥‥？
English: And if your mother doesn't come back, then what...?

Japanese: 里のオキテでは、 あたしが家元になるのかな。
English: Then according to the laws of the village, I'll become the next Master.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんが、家元にねえ‥‥。
English: Mystic Maya... The Master of the Kurain Technique... Sounds like a heavy responsibility.

Japanese: 他にいないからね。綾里本家の 血を受けついでいる霊媒師。
English: Yeah, but there's no one else with the blood of the Fey main family who's a spirit medium.

Japanese: かなりカンシャしてるよね、 なるほどくんに。
English: She certainly seems grateful to you, Nick.

Japanese: タイヘンだったからね。 あの事件は。
English: Well, she was in a pretty tight spot during that terrible ordeal.

Japanese: ぼくも彼女には カンシャしてるよ。
English: Actually, I'm grateful to her, too.

Japanese: うう‥‥なんか ズルいなあ、なるほどくん。
English: Hmmm... It sounds like you're not telling me something.

Japanese: 今度、こっそり見てみようかな。 その事件の記録‥‥。
English: I'm going to take a good look at the case file next chance I get...

Japanese: ごめんね。はみちゃんが あんなコト言っちゃって。
English: I'm sorry that Pearly said what she did.

Japanese: べ、ベツに、気にしてないよ。
English: Oh, it's fine... It-It was nothing...

Japanese: （あとで、仲なおり しておかないとな‥‥）
English: (I'm going to have to smooth things over with Pearls later somehow...)

Japanese: 千尋さんがいなかったら、 今のぼくはなかったよ。
English: If it wasn't for Mia, I wouldn't be the person I am today.

Japanese: ホント、たよりなかったもんねー。 ムカシのなるほどくん。
English: Yeah, she told me you used to really be useless.

Japanese: あまりにたよりなかったから、 今、スゴくたのもしく見えるもん。
English: Compared to how unreliable you were then, you actually seem responsible now.

Japanese: ‥‥じつは、そんなに 変わってないのに。
English: ...Actually, you haven't changed that much, I guess.

Japanese: （ホメられた‥‥ワケじゃ なさそうだな）
English: (Maya praised me for a change! Hey, wait a second...)

Japanese: いつからこんなギザギザな アタマなの？
English: When did you first get that porcupine cut?

Japanese: ‥‥小さいころから こんな感じだったけど。
English: ...Um, it's been like this ever since I was a little kid.

Japanese: ええッ！　そのヘアスタイル、 天然ものだったんだ！
English: What!? You mean that's the way your hair is naturally!?

Japanese: あたしはまた、トコヤさんで 『なるほどカットで』‥‥とか、
English: I thought you had some sort of special "Phoenix Cut" deal with the barber.

Japanese: トクベツ注文してるのかと 思ってたよ。
English: I mean... You usually only see hair like that in a video game.

Japanese: （そんなにヘンかな、 このアタマ‥‥）
English: (Is my hair really that weird-looking?)

Japanese: ‥‥あたしも、もっと シッカリしないとね。
English: I'm going to have to really buckle down and get serious.

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道・家元の ムスメだもんな。
English: Well, you're the future Master of the Kurain Channeling Technique, after all.

Japanese: そうだよ。‥‥それに、 お姉さんがわりだからね。
English: Yeah... That, and I have to play the part of big sister, too.

Japanese: はみちゃんと、なるほどくんの。
English: For Pearly... and you, Nick!

Japanese: ぼ、ぼくもかよ。
English: Wha-?? Do I look like I was born yesterday!?

Japanese: 倉院のツボ‥‥見つかったと 思ったのになー。
English: You know, I thought we'd finally found the urn.

Japanese: 優作くんが仮面マスクなら、 ゼンブ解決なのに。
English: Yeah, if only Ron DeLite were Mask☆DeMasque, we'd have figured it all out.

Japanese: え。優作さん、 仮面マスクじゃないの？
English: Huh? You mean he's NOT Mask☆DeMasque?

Japanese: だって、まれかさんは ちがうって言ってたぞ。
English: Well, Desirée says he isn't and I believe her.

Japanese: ううん‥‥ナゾは深まる ばかりだねえ。
English: Hmm... The mystery gets deeper and deeper.

Japanese: ‥‥裁判は明日なんだけどな。
English: Yeah, and the trial starts tomorrow... as usual.

Japanese: これ‥‥ホンモノの 仮面マスクが作ったんだよな。
English: So this was made by the real Mask☆DeMasque, huh?

Japanese: イトノコ刑事がタイコ判を 押してたからね。
English: Yup, Detective Gumshoe said it was the real thing.

Japanese: “このエンブレムこそ、 ホンモノの証拠ッス”って。
English: "One look at this mark and you know it's the real deal, pal."

Japanese: あの刑事のタイコ判は、 三文判よりチカラ不足だけどね。
English: Yeah, but you do realize whose deductive reasoning skills we're talking about, right...?

Japanese: このカタナ‥‥、 フシギなオーラを感じるね。
English: This sword... It has a strange aura surrounding it.

Japanese: なんというか、怒りや悲しみが こめられているみたいな。
English: It's like it's infused with a spirit of both anger and tragedy.

Japanese: クニャッ、て曲げられてるからね。 怒ってるんじゃないか、カタナも。
English: Well, I'd be mad too if somebody bent me the way they bent this sword.

Japanese: ‥‥ま。そんなトコかも しれないけど。
English: Yeah, I guess that could be it.

Japanese: どう思う？　これ‥‥
English: What do you think about this?

Japanese: うーん‥‥事件に カンケイありそうだけど‥‥
English: Hmmm... It seems like it has something to do with this case...

Japanese: ちょっと、あたしには わからないな。
English: But I'm not really sure...

Japanese: また出てきたね、イトノコさん。
English: Detective Gumshoe's on this case too, huh.

Japanese: 事件といえば、あの刑事だよ。 ‥‥グーゼンとは思えないな。
English: He seems to show up during all of our cases. It can't be a coincidence.

Japanese: ウンメイの赤いイトで しばりつけられてるんじゃない？
English: Maybe it's a sign that our fates are destined to be woven together for all time!

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっとイヤだな、それ。
English: Yeah, that'd be a real Beauty and the Beast kind of fairy tale, wouldn't it.

Japanese: 仮面マスクの正体は‥‥
English: So the real identity of Mask☆DeMasque is...

Japanese: 優作さん‥‥なんだよね？
English: ...Ron DeLite, right?

Japanese: 本人はそう言ってるけどね。 ‥‥どうもハッキリしないんだよ。
English: That's what Mr. DeLite claims. ...But I'm not too convinced about something he said.

Japanese: え。‥‥何が？
English: Huh? About what?

Japanese: 倉院のツボだよ。
English: About the Sacred Urn.

Japanese: 優作くん、どうもツボのこと、 アイマイだろ？
English: Didn't he sound a little vague on the subject?

Japanese: ああ。“なくしちゃった” とか言ってたっけ。
English: Yeah, now that you mention it, he said that he had simply "lost it", right?

Japanese: （何か、まだヒミツがありそうな 気がするんだよな‥‥）
English: (I have this distinct feeling that he's hiding something. I just know it...)

Japanese: いやー、ビックリしたね、 アイガ探偵。
English: That Detective Atmey guy really surprised me.

Japanese: “ズヴァリ！”‥‥だもん。 ちょっとビビっちゃった。
English: There was this certain something about the way he said, "Zvarri!"

Japanese: でも、前回の怪人の犯行で、 エモノを取り戻したんだろ？
English: Well he did manage to get back the treasure that the Mask guy stole last time, right?

Japanese: しかも、たったひとりで。
English: And he did it by himself, too.

Japanese: うん。‥‥その事件で 有名になったからね。
English: Yeah, I guess. ...That case has certainly made him famous.

Japanese: となると、やっぱり‥‥ メイタンテーなのかな。
English: It looks like he does deserve to be called "Ace Detective" after all.

Japanese: カッコイイよねー、まれかさん。 バイクでツッ走るんだよ。
English: That Desirée sure is cool! I love that whole motorcycle thing!

Japanese: ‥‥それも、死のセトギワで。 あたしもやってみようかなー。
English: The way she laughs in the face of death... I want to try it myself!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん、自転車しか 乗れないだろ？
English: I thought you only knew how to ride a bike...

Japanese: でも、でも！　電動自転車 だからね、いちおう。
English: Hey! Don't forget, Nick, that it's an electric bike!

Japanese: ‥‥若いんだから、 ちゃんと運動しろよ！
English: ...E-Electric? So young and already so lazy. Whatever happened to good old exercise?

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: October 12 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: ウエルカムッス！ よーこそ留置所へ！
English: Hey, pal! Welcome to the detention center!

Japanese: ゴキゲンですね、イトノコ刑事‥‥
English: You're sure in a happy mood, aren't you?

Japanese: そりゃそッス。なにしろ、 あの仮面マスクめを‥‥
English: Yup. After all, we got Mask☆DeMasq--

Japanese: それより、あの。 ‥‥ツボのほうは！
English: Right, but... Um... So where's the Sacred Urn?

Japanese: お。‥‥これは、すまねッス。 よくよく考えてみれば‥‥
English: Oh... Sorry about that, pal... I guess I didn't really think about it before...

Japanese: アンタはその。 いわば被害者だったッスな。
English: But you're the "victim" in this case.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: ま。とにかく、本人のクチから ハナシを聞いてほしいッス。
English: Well, I guess I should let you guys hear the story straight from the horse's mouth.

Japanese: そーだよ、なるほどくん！
English: You know, he's right, Nick!

Japanese: な、なんだよ。
English: He is?

Japanese: よおおおく考えたら、 あたしたちって被害者じゃない！
English: If you think about it reeeeal hard, we are the victims!

Japanese: まあ‥‥ある種、ね。
English: Well... I guess so... In a way...

Japanese: あの‥‥
English: Umm...

Japanese: いつも殺人事件だから、被害者って いえば死体だと思ってたケドさ。
English: I'm so used to thinking of the victim as a dead person 'cause we're always on murder cases.

Japanese: スミマセン‥‥
English: Ex-Excuse me...

Japanese: くっそー。なんとしても、 あたしたちのツボを‥‥
English: But anyway! I have to get the Sacred Urn back!

Japanese: ボクのコト、 ムシしないでくださーーーい！
English: Pleeeeease! Don't just ignore meeeeee!
...Err.

Japanese: あの。あなたは？
English: Um, who are you?

Japanese: ‥‥あ。あ。あの。ぼく。その。
English: ...I, err, umm. I-I-I...

Japanese: か、か、か。怪人☆仮面マスク？ ‥‥なんですけれども。
English: I-I'm Ma-Ma-Ma... Mask☆DeMasque...? ...O-OK?

Japanese: なんかショボいよ、なるほどくん。
English: Hey, Nick... Doesn't he look kind of... Well, gangly?

Japanese: 天杉　優作（あますぎゆうさく） です。仮面マスクでおなじみの。
English: I'm Ron DeLite... Th-The famous Mask☆DeMasque.

Japanese: （‥‥イタイタしいな。 なんとも言えず‥‥）
English: (...I don't think this guy could punch his way out of a wet paper bag.)

Japanese: 怪人・仮面マスクなんですか？ ‥‥あなたが。
English: So, you're the Masque DeMask guy?

Japanese: は、はい！　そうです！ ‥‥‥‥いや。ちがうか。
English: Y-Yes! You bet! No... Err, that's wrong.

Japanese: なんていうか、ビミョーだしな。 そのへんは自分でもハッキリしない
English: You see... it's complicated. Sort of yes, and sort of no. If you know what I mean

Japanese: え？　ちがうのですか？
English: Hm? What's wrong?

Japanese: ちがうって言うか、イチガイには なんとも言えないのかな？
English: Well when I say "wrong", what I really mean to say is that it's "not right", per se.

Japanese: そもそも“怪人・”じゃなくて “怪人☆”だし、そのへんをボクは
English: You see it's not spelled "Masque DeMask", it's actually "Mask☆DeMasque"...

Japanese: ‥‥なんなの、このヒト。
English: ...What's with this guy?

Japanese: （とりあえず、気の弱い ヒトであることはたしかだな）
English: (Well, he's a timid little weakling, we know that much.)

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥あわれむような目で 見ないでくださーーーい！
English: D-Don't do that... Don't look at me like that! I don't need your pityyyyy!

Japanese: （そして‥‥ちょっぴり 怒りっぽいみたいだ）
English: (And a major crybaby, too.)

Japanese: でも‥‥どうして 自首したんですか？
English: So... Why did you give yourself up?

Japanese: そ‥‥それは‥‥その。
English: Well, you see... It's because... Well...

Japanese: アレでしょ！　あまりにも 貴重な秘宝を盗んじゃったから、
English: I know why! It's because you stole such a priceless treasure!

Japanese: そのツミの重さに 耐えられなくなったんだよ！
English: And now you're overwhelmed with guilt. Am I right!?

Japanese: （“貴重な秘宝”‥‥って、 あのコワれそうなボロツボか？）
English: ("Priceless treasure"? That broken old urn...?)

Japanese: あの‥‥おねがいが あるんですケド‥‥
English: Umm... I have a little favor to ask...

Japanese: は、はあ。‥‥なんですか？
English: Y-Yes? What is it?

Japanese: まれかちゃんに‥‥ 会っていただけませんか？
English: Could you... go and talk to Dessie?

Japanese: ‥‥ま、まれかちゃん‥‥？
English: ...D-Dessie?

Japanese: とりあえず、返してください！ あたしたちのツボ。
English: Look, we really want our urn back, so where is it?

Japanese: スミマセン！　かならず お返しします！　‥‥‥‥いや。
English: I'm sorry! I'll give it back! I really will! ...Err, maybe not.

Japanese: ダメか。そんな、できもしない ヤクソクは、ヒトをキズつけるだけ
English: No, I can't... I can't make promises I can't keep... I'll just end up hurting people

Japanese: あの‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Um, Mr. Nick...?

Japanese: わたくし、聞き取りにくいです。 ‥‥特に、後半部分が。
English: I couldn't hear what he just said... Especially the last part.

Japanese: ちょっと‥‥仮面マスクさん。 もっと大きな声でおねがいします。
English: Um, Mr. DeMasque? Do you think you could speak up a little?

Japanese: は。は。は。は。はい、 わかりました！　‥‥‥‥いや。
English: Y-Y-Y-Yes, sir! I will... I promise! ...Err, maybe not.

Japanese: でも、待てよ。 ボク、もともとホソい声がウリだし
English: You see, I'm already speaking in my normal voice, so I really can't do anything

Japanese: 仮面マスクさん。 ツボなんですけど‥‥
English: Mr. DeMasque? About the urn...

Japanese: は、はい！　そのコトですけど！ ‥‥‥‥いや。ムダだよな。
English: Oh! Yes! The urn! Right away! ...Err, actually no.

Japanese: こんなボクが何を言ったって 信じてもらえるワケ、ないし、ボク
English: I wouldn't mind telling you, but actually I'm afraid you wouldn't believe me anyway

Japanese: もお！　ハッキリしてください！
English: Enough already! Just tell it to us straight!

Japanese: 大きな声、 出さないでくださーーーい！
English: Please... Please don't yell at meeee!

Japanese: はあ。はあ。はあ。ボク‥‥ボク。
English: *huff huff huff* I... I... I'm sorry...

Japanese: その、倉院のツボを‥‥ なくしちゃいましたーーーッ！
English: That urn... I... I-I lost it!

Japanese: な‥‥‥‥ナニぃぃぃぃぃッ！
English: Wh-Wha-What!?

Japanese: あの。さっき、なんて言いました？ “ツボをなくしちゃった”とか‥‥
English: What did you just say? Did you say you "lost it"?

Japanese: その。はい！　いえ。その‥‥ はい！　やっちゃいましたっ！
English: Yes... Er, no. Umm... Maybe so. I mean, yes, I lost it!

Japanese: ど、どういうコトですか‥‥？
English: Wh-What do you mean...?

Japanese: あの‥‥ホラ。 よくあるじゃないですか。
English: Well, you know... I lost it, like I said... These things just happen...

Japanese: 考えごとしてたら、電車の あみだなに、ニモツを忘れたり。
English: You're on the train and you get distracted and you forget your bag. You know...

Japanese: あみだなに忘れたんですか！ ‥‥あたしたちのツボ！
English: What!? You forgot our Sacred Urn on a train!?

Japanese: それ以前に、そのカッコウで 電車に乗ったんですか！
English: Wait a minute! You went on the train dressed like that!?

Japanese: い、いえいえ！　今のは “たとえば”のハナシですから。
English: N-No! I... I... I was just speaking hypothetically, that's all.

Japanese: と、とにかく‥‥
English: A-Anyway...

Japanese: すみまっせぇぇぇん！ どっか行っちゃいましたーーーッ！
English: I'm sooooorry! I just lost it somewhere! I don't know where!

Japanese: （‥‥どうも、ハナシが アヤフヤだなあ‥‥）
English: (This guy's story is more surreal than a Dali painting.)

Japanese: ホントにコレ、怪人なのかな。
English: Are you really sure this creep is Mask☆DeMasque?

Japanese: ‥‥“コレ”って言うなよ。
English: Maya, don't call him a "creep".

Japanese: （《アヤしいヒト》というイミでは じゅうぶん《怪人》だよな‥‥）
English: (Although I do have to admit, I understand your doubts about this guy...)

Japanese: まれかちゃん‥‥というのは？
English: So who is this Dessie person?

Japanese: き‥‥。気やすく奥さんの名前を 呼ばないでくださーーーい！
English: Pleeeease don't talk about my wife like that!

Japanese: ‥‥ど、どうやら、奥さん みたいだね。‥‥怪人さんの。
English: ...I-I guess she must be his wife...

Japanese: はあ。はあ。はあ‥‥スミマセン。 じ、じつは‥‥
English: *huff huff huff* I'm sorry! The truth is...

Japanese: みなさんに来てもらったのは、 彼女が“そうしろ”って‥‥
English: It was my wife that told me to have you all come here...

Japanese: あの。ボクのアジトに 行ってみてもらえますか？
English: Umm... Do you think you could go and visit her at my hideout?

Japanese: ‥‥あ、あじと‥‥？
English: H... Hideout?

Japanese: なるほどくん！　“あじと”と いうのは、いかなる‥‥？
English: Mr. Nick! What does he mean by "hideout"...?

Japanese: あ。スミマセン。 もちろん、ヒミツ基地のコトです。
English: Oh, sorry. Naturally, I meant to say my "secret base".

Japanese: ‥‥ひ、ひみつきち‥‥？
English: S-Secret Base?

Japanese: ‥‥いわゆる、ボクの自宅です。 《パラッツォ・ペルペペ》３階‥‥
English: Well, my apartment actually. Palazzo Pelpepe, 3rd floor...

Japanese: 行ってみようよ！　なるほどくん！ いざ、怪人のアジトへ！
English: Let's go have a look, Nick! To Mask☆DeMasque's hideout!

Japanese: （“アジト”というヒビキが 気に入ったみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Something tells me she just likes the sound of the word "hideout".)

Japanese: ‥‥あ、そうだ。 奥さんが、これを‥‥
English: ...Oh, that's right! Here. This is from your wife.

Japanese: え！　まれかちゃんが！ ‥‥いただきますッ！
English: Oh? From Dessie!? Thank you!

Japanese: 手紙を怪人にわたした。 ‥‥というか、取られた。
English: Letter given to Mask☆DeMasque. Actually, more like "taken".

Japanese: “ユーサクくん、お元気ですか？ あたしは元気です”‥‥
English: "My dear Ronnie, how are you? I'm doing fine..."

Japanese: （部屋のスミっこにしゃがみこんで 一心に読みふけっている‥‥）
English: (He's clutching onto that letter so hard, the ink is going to be squeezed out...)

Japanese: シアワセそうだねー、怪人さん。
English: He looks so happy...

Japanese: なるほどくんも、真宵さまに お手紙を出しましょうよ！
English: You should write a letter to Mystic Maya too, Mr. Nick!

Japanese: ホント、おさわがせして スミマセン。
English: I'm really sorry I caused so much trouble.

Japanese: あやまるぐらいなら、 最初から盗まなきゃいいのに。
English: If you're so sorry, then you shouldn't have stolen it in the first place!

Japanese: あ‥‥でも。なくしちゃった だけですから！　‥‥‥‥いや。
English: B-But... The only thing I did wrong was lose it! ...Err, that's not right.

Japanese: おんなじコトだよな。 他の秘宝だって、きっと持ちヌシは
English: I was wrong either way. I'm sure the owners of the other treasures feel the same

Japanese: ‥‥反省してるよ。
English: ...He sure seems sorry.

Japanese: ココロのキレイな 怪人さんなのですね‥‥。
English: Who would have imagined that such a famous thief would have such a pure heart...

Japanese: あ。仮面マスクの 予告状じゃないですか！
English: Oh! That's Mask☆DeMasque's calling card!

Japanese: ちょっとシャレてますよね！
English: Pretty nice-looking, huh!?

Japanese: うれしそうだよ、怪人さん。
English: He looks so happy...

Japanese: ボク、文章を書くのが ニガテだから‥‥、
English: I'm not very good at writing, you see...

Japanese: 『ビジネス文書の書き方』っていう 本を買って、勉強したんです！
English: So I bought a book called "How to Write Business Letters" and studied hard!

Japanese: （まあ、これもある種、 “ビジネス”ではあるな‥‥）
English: (Well, I guess you could call thievery a type of business...)

Japanese: どうですか？　これなんですけど。
English: What do you think about this?

Japanese: そういうのはボク、ちょっと。 ‥‥‥‥いや。
English: Hmm... I don't really think I can help you with that. Err, no, that's not it...

Japanese: ちょっとちがうか。 “そういうの”って、どういうのか
English: Well... The thing is... I don't really know if I can say one way or the other

Japanese: よく聞こえないね。
English: I didn't really catch that.

Japanese: 聞くイミもなさそうだよ。
English: Don't worry, you didn't miss anything.

Japanese: キレイですよね！ ボクの奥さんなんですよ！
English: That's my lovely wife! Isn't she beautiful?

Japanese: はあ、そうですね‥‥
English: Yeah, she sure is...

Japanese: ‥‥じろり。
English: *glare*

Japanese: （春美ちゃんの前では、あまり 触れたくない話題だな‥‥）
English: (Whoo boy. The one topic I didn't want to get into in front of Pearls...)

Japanese: でも‥‥。
English: But...

Japanese: まれかちゃん、曲がったコトが キライだから‥‥
English: Dessie doesn't approve of my... "interests".

Japanese: ボク、もう‥‥ キラわれちゃったよなあ。
English: So I'm afraid she doesn't like me anymore...

Japanese: どうすればいいんだろ。 ボクがもっとシッカリやっていれば
English: What should I do? If I want to win her back, I'm really going to have to be super honest

Japanese: （自分の世界に 閉じこもってしまった‥‥）
English: (Oh boy, he's gone back into his own little world again...)

Japanese: 小さいころからのアコガレ だったんですよ！　怪人。
English: Ever since I was a little boy, I've always been interested in mysterious masked men!

Japanese: ユメがかなったワケですか。
English: So that's what you wanted to be when you grew up?

Japanese: はい！　‥‥‥‥いや。 でも、これでよかったのかな‥‥
English: Yes! ...Err, no. Sort of, but not exactly...

Japanese: こうして、秘宝を盗まれて 悲しむヒトだっているのに、ボクは
English: I never imagined I'd feel as bad as I do now about the treasures being stolen...

Japanese: （ううん‥‥ニンゲンって フクザツだよなあ）
English: (...People sure can be complicated, can't they.)

Japanese: そ、そのヒトは‥‥
English: H-Him...!

Japanese: 知ってますよね？　あなたの “好敵手にしてライヴァル”とか。
English: Do you know him? He claims to be your arch-enemy and rival.

Japanese: そ‥‥そんなヒト‥‥
English: Th-That person...

Japanese: 好敵手でもライヴァルでも ありませーーん！　‥‥‥‥いや。
English: He... He's not my arch-enemy OR my rival! ...Well, actually, no.

Japanese: どうなんだろ。“好敵手”も “ライヴァル”も同じイミだしなあ
English: Now that I think about it, the terms arch-enemy and rival are the same thing.

Japanese: いやー、やっぱりカタキどうし、 感情のスレちがいがあるんだねー。
English: They ARE rivals! Only people who have it out for each other would disagree like this!

Japanese: あの‥‥弁護士さん。
English: Umm... Mr. Lawyer?

Japanese: はい？
English: Yes?

Japanese: まれかちゃんの手紙に 書いてあるんですケド‥‥
English: In the letter that Dessie wrote, she said...

Japanese: “このヒトにベンゴを依頼せよ” ‥‥って。
English: "Ask this guy to be your lawyer!"
Huh...?

Japanese: ‥‥あのお。タイヘン あつかましいんですけど‥‥
English: Err, umm... I know this would be asking a lot, but...

Japanese: 引き受けてもらえませんか？ ボクの裁判、明日なんですケド。
English: Could you please take my case? My trial starts tomorrow...

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと！　ベンゴなんて、 できるワケないじゃないですか！
English: Hey, hang on a second! He can't be your lawyer!

Japanese: どうして？
English: Why not?

Japanese: “どうして”じゃないよ！
English: What do you mean, "why not"!?

Japanese: あたしたち、被害者なんだよ？ この怪人さんの！
English: We're the victims in this case, right!? Victims of this DeMasque guy!

Japanese: でも‥‥まれかさんによれば、 優作くんは怪人じゃなくて‥‥
English: Well, yeah... But according to Desirée, he didn't do it...

Japanese: “そう思いこんでいる、カワイソウ なヒト”‥‥というコトでした。
English: She said, "Poor man, he's deluded himself into thinking he did it." Right, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: ダメだよ！　そんなの、 ウソかもしれないじゃない！
English: Come on! She could be lying to protect her husband!

Japanese: そ、そうだな。‥‥‥‥いや。 でも待てよ。むしろここは、やはり
English: W-Well, that's true. ...Err, no actually not. Actually it's hard to say

Japanese: （‥‥って、ウツっちまったぞ！）
English: (...Oh no, it's spreading!)

Japanese: お、おねがいします！ お礼は、お好きな秘宝を１つ‥‥
English: Oh, please! I'll give you the treasure of your choice in return...

Japanese: （‥‥さて‥‥どうしよう）
English: (Hmm... What should I do?)

Japanese: ダメだよ、なるほどくん！ 優作さん、怪人なんだから。
English: Don't do it, Nick! He's a thief!

Japanese: なるほどくん！　わたくしも そう思います！
English: Mr. Nick! I agree with Mystic Maya!

Japanese: （優作くんのタイド‥‥ 生まれながらにアヤしいからな）
English: (Well, I admit something about this guy stinks like last week's sushi.)

Japanese: （ここは、断るべきかもしれない。 でも‥‥）
English: (I guess I really should say no. But...)

Japanese: （ぼくが断ったら‥‥彼には 味方がいなくなってしまう！）
English: (If I do that, this poor guy will have nobody on his side!)

Japanese: ‥‥いいでしょう。 弁護を引き受けます。
English: ...Well, Mr. DeLite, I've decided to take your case.

Japanese: ‥‥ホントですかッ！
English: Really? You will?

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 やってみましょう‥‥弁護。
English: ...Well, Mr. DeLite, I've decided to give it a try. I'll defend you.

Japanese: ‥‥ホントですかッ！
English: Really? You will?

Japanese: なな、ナニ言ってるの なるほどくん！
English: Hey! What're you doing, Nick!?

Japanese: か‥‥かいじん、なのですよ！ この殿方は！
English: He... He's a thief! You can't trust him!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥怪人かもしれない。
English: Well, he may be a thief...

Japanese: でも‥‥どうも、 それだけじゃない気がするんだよ。
English: But I think there's more to this case than meets the eye.

Japanese: な‥‥なるほどくん！ わたくし、見そこないましたっ！
English: M-Mr. Nick...! I-I was wrong about you... I shouldn't have trusted you!

Japanese: は、春美ちゃん‥‥？
English: P-Pearls...?

Japanese: イトしい真宵さまのカタキを 弁護するなんて‥‥
English: I can't believe you'd defend this person after what he did to Mystic Maya...!

Japanese: わたくし‥‥わたくし‥‥ ゼッタイ、ユルせませんっ！
English: I-I... I'll never forgive you... EVER!

Japanese: うわああああああああああああん！
English: WAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAHHH!

Japanese: あ‥‥ちょっと！ はみちゃん！
English: P-! Pearly...! Wait!

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥）
English: (This is going to be ugly...)

Japanese: あたし‥‥ちょっと、見てくるね！
English: I-I'll go after her!

Japanese: あの‥‥スミマセン。 ボクなんかのために‥‥
English: Umm... Sorry about that. I didn't mean to cause any trouble...

Japanese: まあ‥‥ そういうコトになりましたから。
English: Well... You couldn't have known she'd react like that.

Japanese: （‥‥とにかく、 調査してみるしかないな‥‥）
English: (I guess I might as well start investigating...)

Japanese: あ。真宵ちゃん‥‥ ‥‥春美ちゃんは？
English: Oh, Maya... Where's Pearls?

Japanese: 事務所に帰ってるって。 あとで、カオ出してあげてね。
English: She said she's going back to the office. Go check in on her later, would you?

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃんは‥‥いいの？
English: And what about you...? Are you OK with me taking Mr. DeLite's case...?

Japanese: ううん‥‥あたしはね。 なるほどくんを信じてるから。
English: Y-Yeah... I'm fine, Nick. I believe in you.

Japanese: （泣かせるなあ）
English: *sniffle* (I think I'm going to cry.)

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥ボク、取り調べが ありますから‥‥
English: Umm... I, err... I know you have a lot of work to do...

Japanese: よろしくおねがいしますね。
English: I... I really appreciate it.

Japanese: よし！　いっちょ、やるよ。 なるほどくん！
English: OK, Nick! Let's get this show on the road!

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: 怪人を目の前にして、 ややキンチョーぎみに見える。
English: He looks nervous being this close to an alleged thief.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile, you're on candid camera.

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: どうやら今は、 することがないのだろう。
English: It looks like he doesn't have much to do right now.

Japanese: 向かい側のカメラに向かって おもしろいカオをしている。
English: I can tell because he's looking at the camera and making funny faces.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile, you're on candid camera.

Japanese: どうやら今は、 監視するものがないのだろう。
English: The camera doesn't seem to have a lot to tape right now.

Japanese: 向かい側の看守を やさしく見守っている。
English: The only thing it's watching now is that guard over there.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 怪人☆仮面マスク　アジト
English: October 12 Mask☆DeMasque's Hideout

Japanese: なんというか、スゴいね。
English: Wow, check this place out!

Japanese: ダレがどう見たって、 怪人のアジトだね、こりゃ。
English: No doubt about it, this is Mask☆DeMasque's hideout alright.

Japanese: だれー？　ユーサクくん？
English: Who is it? Is that you, Ronnie?

Japanese: あれー。なに？　アナタたち。
English: Huh? Who are you people?

Japanese: あ、あの。奥さん‥‥ まれかさん、ですか‥‥？
English: Err, umm... Are you Desirée DeLite?

Japanese: そうよ。天杉　希華（まれか） ‥‥ああ！
English: That's right. I'm Desirée. Ah!

Japanese: アナタたち、アレだね。 弁護士さんと、被害者さん！
English: Oh! You must be the lawyer... and the victim!

Japanese: は、はい。ご主人に言われて、 お話を聞きに来たんですけど‥‥
English: Y-Yeah, that's us. Your husband told us that you wanted us to stop by.

Japanese: 弁護士の成歩堂　龍一と、 被害者の綾里　真宵です！
English: I'm Maya Fey, and this is Phoenix Wright, attorney at law.

Japanese: へえー。リューイチくんと マヨイちゃんかあ。
English: "Nicky boy" and Maya, huh?

Japanese: いいトコあるねー。ユーサクくんの 言うこと、聞いてくれるなんてさ。
English: I can't tell you how grateful I am that you decided to help my Ronnie.

Japanese: いや、まあ‥‥あははは。
English: Well, um... Aha ha... You're welcome.

Japanese: （“リューイチくん”てなあ‥‥）
English: (Did she really call me "Nicky boy"?)

Japanese: ね‥‥。ちょっといいかな、 リューイチくん。
English: Say... Can I ask you something, Nicky boy?

Japanese: なんですか？
English: What is it?

Japanese: アタシね。たしかに、 イイカゲンな女だけどさ。
English: I know I may seem like a "bad girl" on the outside...

Japanese: 曲がったコトだけは、 ガマンできないんだよねー。
English: But the one thing I won't stand for is illegal activity.

Japanese: そんな感じですよね！ なんか、“アネゴ”みたいな。
English: I had the feeling you didn't! You're tough... But I can tell you've got a good heart.

Japanese: だれかが、ユーサクくんを ワナにかけたんだよ。
English: Somebody framed my poor Ronnie, I just know it.

Japanese: あの‥‥これ。ユーサクくんに わたしてくれないかな。
English: Umm... Could you give this to Ronnie for me?

Japanese: お手紙、ですか‥‥？
English: A letter?

Japanese: ユーサクくんにね。 たたかってもらいたいんだ。
English: Yes, for Ronnie. I want him to fight back.

Japanese: まれかさん‥‥
English: Ms. DeLite...

Japanese: わかりました！　安心して、 あたしたちにまかせてください！
English: OK, you've got it! Just relax and leave it all to us!

Japanese: 《希華の手紙》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Desirée's Letter added to the Court Record.

Japanese: これ‥‥仮面マスクだよ！
English: It's Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: ムネに、テープレコーダーが ブラさがっているな。
English: There's a tape recorder hanging from his neck.

Japanese: どれどれ。いじってみよう！
English: Let me see that!
...click...

Japanese: わははははははははははははは‥‥
English: Aha ha ha ha ha ha ha...

Japanese: ‥‥ゴクロウだったねえ、諸君。 宝冠はいただいて行くよ‥‥
English: I'll be taking this crown now! Better luck next time, gentlemen!

Japanese: わーははははははははははは‥‥
English: Aha ha ha ha ha ha ha...

Japanese: これはどういうコトかな、 なるほどくん。
English: I don't get it... What's this supposed to be, Nick?

Japanese: きゃあ、カワイイ！ これ、気球っていうんだよ！
English: Oh, that's so cute! It's called a hot air balloon, you know!

Japanese: ‥‥さすがに知ってるよ。
English: ...Wow! There's no fooling you!

Japanese: あたしも 作ってみようかなー、気球。
English: I want to make my own balloon now!

Japanese: なるほどくんも 乗せてあげるからね！
English: I'll even let you ride in it, Nick!

Japanese: （気球には、ゴクヒのはずの エンブレムがプリントされてる）
English: (That emblem on the balloon is supposed to be top-secret.)

Japanese: （‥‥天杉　優作が仮面マスクで あるという証拠だ）
English: (That's proof enough that Ron DeLite really is Mask☆DeMasque.)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんにはダマっておこう。 ‥‥コワいから）
English: (I'd better not tell Maya. She gets kinda... scary when it comes to "celebrities"...)

Japanese: ね、なるほどくん。 イスの上、見てよ！
English: Hey, Nick! Look on top of the chair!

Japanese: ああ。封筒と‥‥手紙があるね。
English: Looks like an envelope... And a letter.

Japanese: ほらほら。 ちょっと読んでみようよ。
English: Well, don't just stand there! Come on, let's read it!

Japanese: いやいや、マズいだろ、 さすがに。
English: Maya, you know we shouldn't do that!

Japanese: ナニ言ってんの。‥‥ものスゴく 重要な手がかりかもしれないよ！
English: What are you talking about? That letter could be an important clue!

Japanese: ダメだよ。オモシロ半分に ヒトの手紙を読むなんて。
English: No way. You can't just go reading a private letter because you feel like it.

Japanese: ‥‥ちぇ。オモシロ半分だけど、 もう半分はマジメなのにな。
English: ...Hmph! It's not just because I "feel like it". It really could be important!

Japanese: （ハッキリ“手がかりだ”と わかれば、話もちがうけど‥‥）
English: (If I was sure it was a clue, I guess I wouldn't mind...)

Japanese: 中途ハンパなヘンソウ道具が ガクに入ってかざられている。
English: It's a bunch of cheap costume pieces arranged inside a nice frame.

Japanese: つ、つけヒゲだよ なるほどくん！
English: L-Look, Nick! It's a fake beard!

Japanese: 厚紙を切りぬいた 仮面もあるぞ‥‥
English: There's also a mask made out of cardboard...

Japanese: ほらほら、なるほどくん。 つけヒゲつけヒゲ！
English: Here, Nick! Try on this fake beard! Come on! Try it on!

Japanese: う、うわ！ ナニするんだよ‥‥
English: H-Hey! Cut it out...

Japanese: ついでにマスクも かぶるかぶる！
English: Now put on the mask!

Japanese: あはははははははは！ 怪人なるほどくんだ！
English: Aha ha ha ha ha ha! It's Phoenix☆DeMasque!

Japanese: しばらく借りておこうよ。
English: Let's borrow it for a while.

Japanese: （やだよ！）
English: (No way! I'm not stealing from a thief! I have enough bad karma as is!)

Japanese: （‥‥なんか、どこかで 見たことがあるような気がする）
English: (Hmmm... This thing looks vaguely familiar.)

Japanese: あ！　金魚ばちがあるよ。 逆さまだけど。
English: Oh, I know! It's a fishbowl! Well, an upside-down one, anyway...

Japanese: ヘルメットかもしれないよ。 ‥‥宇宙人の。
English: I think it's a helmet. ...An alien helmet.

Japanese: となりにあるのは、 テープレコーダーかな。
English: And that looks like a tape recorder or something next to it.

Japanese: 通信機かもしれないよ。 ‥‥宇宙人の。
English: I think it's a transceiver. ...An alien transceiver.

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Nick...

Japanese: きちんと現実を見つめて、 オトナになろうよ。
English: Do you think maybe you could act like a grown-up for a little bit?

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんに意見された）
English: (Getting lectured by Maya to be more of an adult. That's a new low...)

Japanese: ゴチャゴチャと いろいろ置いてある。
English: There's a whole bunch of stuff piled up over here.

Japanese: み、ミサイルに ダイナマイトがあるよ！
English: Duck and cover! It-It's a missile! Did you know there's d-dynamite in missiles!?

Japanese: この、不吉な重量感‥‥ お、オモチャだよ。よくできた。
English: But they're not heavy enough to be real... Oh wait. They're just really well-made toys.

Japanese: こっちの、ゴミ箱にささっている 巻きものはナニかな？
English: What are these rolls of paper stuck in the trash can?

Japanese: 《マザリンの宝石強奪 アルバトロス大作戦の巻》
English: "Theft of the Mazarin Stone: The Great Albatross Strategy"

Japanese: 《不死身の女神像強奪 ナックルボンバー大作戦の巻》
English: "Theft of the Immortal Goddess Statue: The Great Knucklebomber Strategy"

Japanese: （警察の人たち、ここは 捜査したのかな‥‥）
English: (Did the police even investigate this place...?)

Japanese: 本がドンとつんである。 かなりあるな。
English: There are a bunch of books lying here.

Japanese: ‥‥怪人やら怪盗やらが カツヤクする小説ばかりだ。
English: They're all romantic-sounding novels about famous heists and fantastic thieves.

Japanese: しおりやメモがいっぱい はさんであるよ。
English: Wow! Look at all the bookmarks and notes stuck into them!

Japanese: どれどれ。
English: Let's see here...

Japanese: 《注意：この怪人の笑いかたを 参考にすること》
English: "Note: Must remember this thief's way of laughing. Very useful."

Japanese: 《注意：やっぱり、予告状は 怪人としてのエチケット》
English: "Note: A calling card is an important part of any good thief's modus operandi!"

Japanese: べ、勉強ネッシンだね。 ‥‥優作くん。
English: W-Wow, Ron really studied his stuff!

Japanese: ぎょ‥‥魚雷があるよ、 なるほどくん！
English: Nick! It-It's a torpedo!

Japanese: ‥‥信じがたいけど、 ホンモノっぽいぞ、コレ。
English: ...It's hard to believe, but it sure looks real.

Japanese: まったく‥‥どこから 拾ってくるのかな、こんなの。
English: Where could he have picked up something like this?

Japanese: 落としモノみたいに言うなよ。
English: Please don't say that torpedoes are things people just leave lying around.

Japanese: あの‥‥ご主人ですけど。
English: Umm, about your husband...

Japanese: ふふっ。カワイイでしょ？ ちょっぴり、たよりないけど。
English: Hee hee, isn't he the cutest? A bit weak sometimes though...

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっぴり、ね。
English: ..."A bit"?

Japanese: あのね。テレビでマヨイちゃんの コト、紹介してたんだよ。
English: By the way, did you know they mentioned Maya on TV?

Japanese: え！　ホントですか！
English: Huh? Really!?

Japanese: ツボの持ち主で、弁護士事務所で 怪しげな修行をしている、って。
English: They said you own the urn and that you're doing some shady training at a law office now.

Japanese: （情報がコンランしているな‥‥）
English: (Remind me not to watch that channel's news program.)

Japanese: せっかくだから、その弁護士さんに 会ってみよう、って思ったんだ。
English: After I saw that, I thought it might be a good idea to meet the lawyer they mentioned.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: ...I see.

Japanese: ユーサクくん、思いこみが はげしくてさー。
English: My Ronnie... He has a powerful imagination and gets deluded easily.

Japanese: どうしても自首するって 聞かなくて。コマっちゃったよ。
English: He kept insisting he was going to surrender to the police. I didn't know what to do!

Japanese: 『あのツボは、ぼくが 盗んだんだー！』‥‥なんて。
English: "It was me! I stole that urn!" he kept saying! As if it was even possible!

Japanese: え‥‥？　で、でも‥‥ ツボは優作さんが‥‥その。
English: Huh...? B-But... Are you saying he didn't?

Japanese: あははははは。 そんなワケないじゃない。
English: Ha ha ha. Of course he didn't!

Japanese: どうしてユーサクくんが そんなもの、盗むの？
English: Why would my Ronnie ever do something like that?

Japanese: だ、だってそれは‥‥その。 アレじゃないですか！
English: W-Well, you know... It's a p-priceless treasure! And-And he's... You know...

Japanese: （‥‥なんだ？　もしかして、 知らないのか？　ご主人の正体）
English: (...Is it possible his own wife doesn't know his secret identity?)

Japanese: アタシね。スリルがないと ダメなオンナなんだよねー。
English: Me? I'm the kind of woman that needs excitement in her life!

Japanese: はああ‥‥すりる、ですか。
English: Oh... Excitement, huh?

Japanese: バイクを飛ばしてるときがね。 アタシ、最高にシアワセだな。
English: Yes. I'm at my happiest when I'm racing along with my bike going at full throttle.

Japanese: ‥‥ホラ。バイクって、つねに 死ぬセトギワでツッ走るじゃない？
English: Riding a motorcycle is like putting your life on the line. You know what I mean?

Japanese: （それは、乗りかたに よると思うけど‥‥）
English: (Well, I think that depends on how you ride it...)

Japanese: タイクツな毎日が 何よりニガテなんだ。アタシ。
English: I'm the type that can't stand living a boring, dreary old life with no action.

Japanese: でも‥‥優作さん、スリルが 好きそうには見えなかったなあ。
English: No offense, but your husband, Ron, doesn't exactly look like the risk-taking type himself.

Japanese: あー、たしかに。彼にスリルを 求めるのはムリだけどね。
English: You're right about that. He's definitely not one for thrills and danger.

Japanese: でもね。ちゃーんと、 かわりのモノをくれるから。
English: But I do have to say that he makes up for it in other ways.

Japanese: かわりのモノ‥‥って？
English: Other ways...?

Japanese: おカネ。‥‥ユーサクくん、 フシギにお金持ちなんだよ。
English: Yes, money... Ronnie is incredibly rich... And super generous.

Japanese: 目玉が飛び出るほど スピードの出るバイクを買って、
English: He bought me a bike that's so fast it would make your head spin.

Japanese: 目玉が飛び出るほど高い ショッピングをするのが好き。
English: And shopping? I like to shop so much it makes Ron's head spin.

Japanese: ユーサクくん、こないだ ホントに飛びだしてたもん。目玉。
English: The other day, it actually happened. His head actually started to spin.

Japanese: （‥‥かなり燃費のワルい 奥さんだな‥‥）
English: (Man, talk about a high maintenance wife...)

Japanese: 優作さん‥‥そんなお金、 どうやってかせいでるんですか？
English: So where does Mr. DeLite get all this money from?

Japanese: ユーサクくん、警備員なんだよ。 死ぬセトギワではたらいてるから。
English: Ronnie is a security guard. They put their lives on the line, right?

Japanese: そりゃあ、お金ぐらい もらわないとねー。がっぽり。
English: So they get paid tons of money in return. Tons of it.

Japanese: （ケイビイン‥‥似合わないなあ）
English: (He's a security guard...? I think it's my turn for my head to spin...)

Japanese: ゆうべのコトを 聞かせてもらえますか？
English: Can you tell us about what happened last night?

Japanese: その事件が起こったの、 何時ごろかな。
English: Hmm, what time did that incident take place again?

Japanese: 何時だっけ？　真宵ちゃん。
English: What time was it again, Maya?

Japanese: イトノコさんの話では、たしか 午前１時３０分‥‥だったよ。
English: Well, according to Detective Gumshoe, it was around 1:30 in the morning.

Japanese: やー、ゆうべはヤバかったなー。 つかまっちゃってさあ。
English: Ah, last night was horrible. I got pulled over!

Japanese: つつ、つかまった？
English: P-Pulled over?

Japanese: アタシね、夜はいつも 飛ばすことにしてるんだけど。
English: Yes... I always do my best flying at night!

Japanese: “飛ばす”‥‥って、 バイクですか、やっぱり。
English: "Flying"...? Um, you're talking about your motorcycle, right?

Japanese: 追いつかれちゃったんだよねー。 ゆうべは、おまわりさんに。
English: Yes. Anyway, last night I got pulled over. By a policeman.

Japanese: あれはなかなか、アツい レースだったよ。
English: I can't believe he caught up to me! It was a great chase, let me tell you!

Japanese: おまわりさん、死にそうな カオ、してたもん。
English: When he finally caught me, the poor man was as white as a sheet.

Japanese: 帰ってきたら、夜中の３時 すぎてたっけ。
English: It was about three in the morning when I finally got home.

Japanese: （アリバイはバッチリ、か）
English: (Sounds like she's got an airtight alibi...)

Japanese: あの。優作さんのほうは‥‥？
English: Well, what about Mr. DeLite?

Japanese: さー。ゆうべは いっしょじゃなかったからなあ。
English: I don't really know... We weren't together at that time of the night.

Japanese: 帰ってきたら、 さすがにもう、寝てたけど。
English: But when I got home, he was already fast asleep.

Japanese: （こっちはアリバイなし、か‥‥）
English: (So basically he's got no alibi...)

Japanese: あの‥‥優作さんは、 怪人☆仮面マスクでは‥‥？
English: So... When did Ron first become Mask☆DeMasque?

Japanese: ナニ言ってんの、リューイチくん。 そんなワケ、ないじゃない！
English: What are you talking about? He's not Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: ‥‥はあ‥‥。 い、いやいや！　待ってください！
English: Huh? But... W-Wait a second!

Japanese: だって、この部屋は、どう見たって 怪人のアジトふう‥‥
English: Look around at this room! This place is obviously Mask☆DeMasque's hideout...

Japanese: 逆だよ、ギャク。
English: No no, you're totally wrong.

Japanese: ホンモノの怪人が、こんな バカな部屋に住んでると思う？
English: A real thief's hideout wouldn't look like this!

Japanese: ユーサクくん、気が弱いから。 アコガレが強いんだ。ヒーローに。
English: It's because Ronnie is so timid himself that he looks up to heroic figures.

Japanese: ひーろー‥‥って、 ワルモノですよ？　仮面マスク。
English: Heroic figures? But Mask☆DeMasque is a bad guy.

Japanese: （“思いこみ”‥‥ ホントにそうなんだろうか‥‥）
English: (So he's "deluded", huh... Could that really be true?)

Japanese: だって、彼が盗んだのなら、 持ってるハズだよね？　そのツボ。
English: Anyway, if he really had stolen the urn, wouldn't he still have it?

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥そうなんですけど。
English: W-Well, I suppose so...

Japanese: ユーサクくんに聞いてみなよ。 ‥‥盗んだツボのありか。
English: Then why don't you ask him if he has the urn or not?

Japanese: （‥‥たしかに‥‥、どうも シックリこないんだよな）
English: (She has a point. Mr. DeLite isn't exactly the criminal type...)

Japanese: （優作くんの、あのタイド‥‥）
English: (But something about Ron's behavior bothers me.)

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥怪人じゃないかも しれないね、優作さん。
English: Maybe it's true... Maybe Mr. DeLite isn't Mask☆DeMasque after all.

Japanese: たよりなさそうに見えるけどねー。
English: I know he doesn't look it...

Japanese: これでも、やるときは ビシッとやるんだよ。
English: But he can really get things done when he puts his mind to it.

Japanese: へえ！　いつなんですか？ その“やるとき”って。
English: Really? So when exactly does he put his mind to it?

Japanese: ‥‥まあ。 めったにないけど。
English: ...Well. Not very often, I admit.

Japanese: （まれかさん‥‥優作くんの どこにホレたのかな）
English: (What exactly does Ms. DeLite see in her husband anyway?)

Japanese: きっと、ドラマティックな 出会いがあったんだよ！
English: Wow, you must've really seen skyrockets when you first met to love him so much!

Japanese: “ティック”と来たか‥‥
English: Skyrockets...? I didn't know people still used that word.

Japanese: ‥‥あの、まれかさん。 もちろん、知ってますよね？
English: ...Um, Ms. DeLite. You must know, right?

Japanese: ご主人‥‥優作さんが、 あの怪人☆仮面マスクだ、って。
English: About your husband and Mask☆DeMasque...

Japanese: ああ。仮面マスクでしょ？ 知ってるよー、モチロン。
English: Mask☆DeMasque? Of course. I know all about it.

Japanese: 大ファンだからね、 ユーサクくん。
English: My husband is his biggest fan.

Japanese: そうですよねー。 ‥‥え？
English: Yup... Wha...? ...Huh?

Japanese: だいふぁん‥‥？
English: B-Biggest fan?

Japanese: ユーサクくん、思いこみが はげしいからさ。
English: Yes, and Ronnie can be pretty delusional sometimes.

Japanese: こんな騒ぎになっちゃって。
English: That's how this whole misunderstanding happened.

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待ってください！ “思いこみ”というのは‥‥？
English: W-Wait a minute! Did you say "delusional"!?

Japanese: それなんだよねー。ホント コマってるんだ、アタシ。
English: Yes, that's right. I just don't know what I'm going to do with him...

Japanese: ユーサクくん、自分が仮面マスク だと思いこんじゃって。
English: You see, Ronnie actually believes that HE is Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: なにィィィィィィィッ！
English: な‥‥<Maya> WH<Maya> -WHAAAAT!?

Japanese: く、詳しく話を聞かせてください！
English: What are you talking about!?

Japanese: たいしたもんだよねー、 その若さで家元なんてさ。
English: That's really amazing! A "Master" at your age!

Japanese: い、いえいえ。今の家元は、 あたしのおかあさんなんですけど。
English: Oh no, you've got it wrong. It's my mother that's the current Master!

Japanese: あ。そうなんだ。 ‥‥ところでさ。なんの家元？
English: Oh, I see... So what exactly is she the master of anyway?

Japanese: えーと、れいば
English: Well, you see, it's li--

Japanese: やめとけよ。“霊媒”なんて 言っても、笑われるだけだぞ。
English: Don't say it, Maya. If you say "spirit channeling", she'll laugh.

Japanese: ううう‥‥さみしいなあ。
English: Ouch... That hurt.

Japanese: リューイチくん、 弁護士なんだよね。
English: So, Nicky boy, you're a lawyer, huh?

Japanese: ええ、まあ。
English: Well, yeah.

Japanese: どう？　弁護士って。 スリルがあるんじゃない？
English: How do you like it? I bet it's SO exciting!

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、たしかに。 不必要なほどスリリングですね。
English: ...Yeah, sometimes. Actually, a little too exciting at times.

Japanese: ‥‥アタシ、大好物 なんだよねー、スリル。
English: Oooh! I just love a good thrill.

Japanese: ‥‥ふうん‥‥いいなあ、 リューイチくんは‥‥
English: Hmm... Nicky boy... It sounds like you have it so nice...

Japanese: （‥‥うう‥‥ いごこち悪いな、どうも）
English: (Um... This is starting to get awkward.)

Japanese: アタシは、バイクとユーサクくんが いれば、シアワセだなー。
English: As long as I've got my motorcycle and my Ronnie, I'm happy.

Japanese: うわあ、ステキですね！
English: Oh! That's so sweet!

Japanese: あとは‥‥少しのスリルと、 少しじゃないお金があれば。
English: Oh, that plus a little excitement and a little shopping money.

Japanese: それで、もうじゅうぶんかな。
English: ...THEN I'm happy.

Japanese: ‥‥意外によくばりだよ、 なるほどくん‥‥
English: Now she's starting to sound a bit greedy... Don't you think, Nick?

Japanese: すみません。 これ、なんですけど‥‥
English: Excuse me, but would you take a look at this...?

Japanese: やー、ゴメン。 アタシ、意外に世間知らずでさー。
English: Oh, I'm sorry... I haven't the faintest idea.

Japanese: ちょっと、わからないな。 そういうの。
English: I guess I just don't get out enough.

Japanese: やー、なんかシンピ的だよね。 アタシ好きだなー、こういうの。
English: Ooh! That's so mysterious looking... I love old stuff like this!

Japanese: ホントですか！　あたしたちの お宝なんですよ！
English: Really!? That's one of our most treasured possessions!

Japanese: 今度、あの高菱屋で 秘宝展をやるんです！
English: There's going to be a whole exhibit at Lordly Tailor with these types of things!

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん、とても うれしそうなカオをしてるぞ）
English: (Maya looks so happy. She's lit up like a 1,000 watt bulb.)

Japanese: 今度、ユーサクくんといっしょに 見に行くね。‥‥その秘宝展。
English: Terrific! You can count on Ronnie and me to be there! Sounds like fun!

Japanese: え！　そ、そうですね‥‥ あははは。
English: Huh! ...Oh, err, yeah... Ha ha ha...

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん、とても フクザツなカオをしているな）
English: (Oops, looks like that light bulb is starting to flicker.)

Japanese: あの、これなんですけど‥‥
English: Umm, so what do you know about this...?

Japanese: だ、ダメだよなるほどくん！
English: No! You can't show her that, Nick!

Japanese: そのエンブレム、ゴクヒだって 言われたでしょ！
English: That emblem is top-secret! Don't you remember!?

Japanese: あ‥‥そうだったっけ。
English: ...Doh! Oops, sorry about that.

Japanese: （ゴクヒと言われると、よけい 見せびらかしたくなるよな‥‥）
English: (But the way she just said "top-secret" was a worse giveaway than what I did...)

Japanese: あたしも、ゴクヒって言われると よけい見たくなるよー。
English: Ooh! When I hear the word "top-secret", it just piques my interest like nothing else!

Japanese: それ、ユーサクくんに わたしてね。
English: Could you please give that to my Ronnie?

Japanese: ダイジなコトが書いてあるから。
English: The contents are very important.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥ 留置所に行ってみるか）
English: (*sigh* Back to the detention center I go...)

Japanese: ね、ね、なるほどくん！ アイガ探偵が言ってたよね！
English: Hey! Nick! Remember what Detective Atmey said!?

Japanese: 天杉　優作は、仮面マスクとして 犯行を重ねる理由があるのです。
English: Exactly that. Mr. DeLite had a very good reason to dress up and commit those crimes.

Japanese: 彼の部屋に、ミドリ色の封書が あるはず。
English: There should be a green envelope somewhere in his room.

Japanese: ‥‥調べてみることですな。
English: ...You'll need to go and investigate.

Japanese: ミドリ色の封書‥‥か。 どうやら、コレみたいだな。
English: A green envelope, huh... Looks like this is it.

Japanese: よおし。読むぞお！
English: OK! Let's have a look!

Japanese: 《お前の正体を世間に 公表されたくなければ‥‥》
English: "If you don't want your true identity revealed to the world...

Japanese: 《１２日の午前１時、５００万円を 用意して、ＫＢ警備会社に来い》
English: ...come to KB Security at 1:00 AM on October 12th and bring $50,000."

Japanese: ‥‥ごごご、ごひゃくまんえん‥‥
English: F-F-F-Fifty thousand...!

Japanese: こ‥‥これって‥‥ キョーハクジョー‥‥だよな。
English: Th-Th... This is a... B-B-Blackmail letter...

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥コイツは ものスゴい手がかりだ‥‥！）
English: (It sure looks like a major clue alright!)

Japanese: 証拠品《脅迫状》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Blackmail Letter added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 天杉　優作は‥‥脅迫状を 受け取っていたんだ。
English: So Ron received a blackmail letter, huh?

Japanese: まれかさんは知ってるのかな？ このコト‥‥
English: Do you think Ms. DeLite knows about this?

Japanese: 知ってたら、ニコニコ笑ってる ワケにも行かないだろ。
English: Well if she did, I don't think she would've been smiling like that.

Japanese: 教えてあげたほうが いいのかな‥‥
English: I wonder if we should tell her about it...

Japanese: よけいなコトは しないほうがいいと思うよ。
English: I think it'd be smarter if we didn't.

Japanese: ‥‥ぴんぽーん‥‥
English: ...*ding-dong*...

Japanese: あれ、またお客さんかあ。 ‥‥ちょっと待っててね。
English: Oh! Someone's at the door! I'll be right back. Won't take but a second, I promise.

Japanese: （やー、わざわざアリガト。 うれしいなー）
English: (Oh, thank you for coming! That's so nice of you!)

Japanese: （なーに。オレ、困ってる美人を 見ると、ほっとけなくてさァ）
English: (When I see a damsel in distress, I just can't help myself!)

Japanese: （ちょっと上がって行ってよ。 コーヒーいれたげるから）
English: (Please, come on in! I'll make some coffee.)

Japanese: （えー、ホントぉ？　じゃ、 ゴチソウになっちゃおうかなぁ）
English: (Really? OK, I guess I'll make myself at home, pretty lady!)

Japanese: （こ、この、ケーハクな声。 どこかで聞いたような‥‥）
English: (...Wait a minute. I know that voice...)

Japanese: リューイチくん、ゴメン！ もう１人、お客さんが来ちゃった。
English: Oh, Nicky boy, I'm so sorry, but I've got another guest!

Japanese: ああああああああッ！ おまえ‥‥おまえ‥‥
English: Aaaaaaaaaaaah!! You... You're...

Japanese: 成歩堂じゃねえか！ それに、マヨイちゃんも！
English: Nick, it's you! And Maya, too! What a fluke!

Japanese: あ‥‥ヤッパリさん。 おひさしぶりです！
English: Ah...! Larry! Long time no see!

Japanese: あれェ？　知り合いなの？ リューイチくんたち。
English: What? You know each other?

Japanese: りゅ‥‥ “リューイチくん”って‥‥
English: Ni... "Nicky boy"!?

Japanese: 成歩堂！　この子、 アンタのなんなのさ？
English: Nick, do you and this girl have... you know... something going on?

Japanese: な、“なんなのさ”と言われても
English: "S-Something"? If you mean what I thin--

Japanese: 見そこなったぜ、成歩堂！
English: I underestimated you, Nick!

Japanese: こんな‥‥美人の人妻に‥‥ ズルいぞおおお成歩堂おおお！
English: A gorgeous lady like this? ...And married, too? Way to go, dude!

Japanese: （やっぱり、メンドウな コトになっちまった‥‥）
English: (I knew it. Just when things can't get any worse, it's time to cue in the Butz.)

Japanese: 矢張　政志（やはりまさし）。 小学校のころからの‥‥
English: Larry Butz... Ever since grade school, he's been...

Japanese: 親友というか、まあ知り合いだ。
English: Not exactly a close friend, but yeah, we know each other.

Japanese: 水くせェな。オマエの初めての 依頼人じゃねえか！
English: Hey, man, that wasn't nice! I was your very first client!

Japanese: “事件のカゲに、ヤッパリ矢張”と 言われつづけて、今年で２５周年。
English: It's been 25 years since I first met Larry.

Japanese: まったくそのとおり。こいつに 関わると、ロクなコトがない。
English: And what we used to say still rings true. When something smells, it's usually the Butz.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだー、そういうワケかい。 ビックリしたぜ。
English: Hey, come on now! You're embarrassing me!

Japanese: やっぱり、人妻はアレよな。 ヤバいって！
English: And in front of this pretty little thing here...

Japanese: ふーん‥‥シンユーか。 いいね、そういうの。
English: So you two are old childhood friends, I see. That's so sweet!

Japanese: 好きなだけしゃべっててよ。 アタシ、バイク見てくるから。
English: You two go ahead and catch up on old times. I'm going to go check on my bike.

Japanese: ‥‥いい子よなー、マレカちゃん。 で、最近どうよ？　成歩堂。
English: Nice girl, that Dessie... So how have ya been, Nick?

Japanese: （‥‥いそがしいんだけどな‥‥）
English: (Too busy if you really want to know...)

Japanese: あ、なるほどくん。電話が‥‥
English: Oh, Nick! Your phone!

Japanese: も‥‥もしもしっ。 成歩堂さんのお宅でしょうか？
English: H-Hello? Is this the Wright residence?

Japanese: あ‥‥春美ちゃん！ 今、どうしてるの？
English: Ah! Pearls! Where are you!?

Japanese: そ、それが‥‥高菱屋さんに 行こうと思ったのですが‥‥
English: I-I thought I'd go to Lordly Tailor to try to find some more clues, but...

Japanese: すっかり、道に 迷ってしまいまして‥‥
English: ...I'm afraid I've gotten lost.

Japanese: えええッ！　ちょっと貸して、 なるほどくん！
English: Whaaaat!? Give me that phone, Nick!

Japanese: は、はみちゃん！ どこにいるの、今！
English: P-Pearly! Where are you right now!?

Japanese: あの‥‥歩いていたら、あの方の 事務所についてしまいました。
English: Um... I was walking along and I found myself in front of that person's office.
"That person"...? Who!?

Japanese: トシのわりに落ちつきのない、 “ズヴァリ！”のオジさまの‥‥
English: Um, that person who doesn't act his age and always says, "Zvarri!" when he's excited...

Japanese: （名探偵・星威岳　哀牙か‥‥）
English: (Luke Atmey, Ace Detective...)

Japanese: そこにいるんだよ、春美ちゃん！ 今、むかえに行くから！
English: OK, stay right there! We're coming to get you!

Japanese: は、はい‥‥。 わたくし、心細くて‥‥
English: A-Alright... I-I'm a little scared...

Japanese: よし！　すぐ行こう、真宵ちゃん！
English: Alright! Let's go, Maya!

Japanese: ちょっと待て、成歩堂！
English: Wait a second, Nick!

Japanese: な、なんだよ。
English: Wh-What?

Japanese: 今の電話！　カワイイ 女の子の声だったぞ。
English: That phone call just now... Sounded like real cutie...

Japanese: その子‥‥アンタのなんなのさ！
English: Another one of your, ahem, ..."special friends"?

Japanese: まれかさんによろしくな、矢張。
English: Say goodbye to Ms. DeLite for me, would you Larry?

Japanese: おまえ、今まで何してたんだ？ たしか、２年ぶりぐらいだよな。
English: So what have you been up to this whole time? It's been two years since I heard from you.

Japanese: いつのまにか、ファッションの センスもおかしくなってるし。
English: And what's with these clothes? You look... well, weird.

Japanese: これは、バイト先の制服だッ！
English: Hey man, that's uncalled for. This is my uniform, from my part-time job.

Japanese: いやー、それにしても２年か。 早いよなァ、時のたつのだけは。
English: But I can't believe it's been two years. They say time flies when you're having fun.
Ha ha ha ha... Ah ha... Ha... ...ha?

Japanese: ど、どうした？
English: Wh-What's wrong?

Japanese: 成歩堂よぉ。‥‥オレ。 もう‥‥オンナなんか信じねぇぞ。
English: Nick... I gotta tell you. Women. ...I don't trust 'em anymore.

Japanese: また、フラれたのか？
English: You got dumped again?

Japanese: あのマキコを追っかけて、オレ チベットへ飛んだワケよ、２年前。
English: Well, you know Bennifer? I followed her all the way to Tibet 2 years ago.

Japanese: そこでオレ、例のコズエと 運命の出会いをしたんだけどサ。
English: That's where I met the famous Katty Tom, and my whole life changed.

Japanese: “あのマキコ”も“例のコズエ”も 聞いたことないぞ。
English: "Bennifer"? "Katty Tom"? I never heard of either of them.

Japanese: でも、コズエのヤツ‥‥オレより ハリウッドをとるわ、だってよォ！
English: Anyway... Dude! Katty Tom chose Hollywood over me! Can you believe it?

Japanese: それは‥‥その。 大ボウケンでしたねえ。
English: Well, actually... Anyway, sounds like you had quite the adventure.

Japanese: オンナなんか‥‥ オンナなんかあああぁぁぁ！
English: Women! They're so... so... Aaaah!

Japanese: あ。‥‥とは言っても、 マヨイちゃんだけはベツだから。
English: Oh... B-But you're different, Maya. You're not like the rest.

Japanese: （‥‥まだコリないらしい）
English: (...Looks like he's still the same old Larry.)

Japanese: で？　なんでおまえ、 こんなトコロへ来たんだ？
English: So what are you doing here anyway?
Huh...?

Japanese: オレってホレ、生まれながらの ナイスガイだろ？
English: What do you mean by that? I'm just a natural-born nice guy, that's all.

Japanese: ‥‥じつは、コレ。ゆうべ、 バイト先で拾っちゃってよ。
English: Well, actually... I picked this up last night while I was working.

Japanese: ‥‥サイフ？ ゆうべ、拾ったって‥‥？
English: ...A wallet? You say you found this last night?

Japanese: 免許証が入ってたからさァ。 これはもう、届けるだろ。フツー。
English: Yeah, it had a driver's license in it, so I figured I'd just return it myself.

Japanese: 証拠品《天杉　優作のサイフ》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Ron's Wallet added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと見せてみろよ、 そのサイフの中身。
English: Hang on... Let me see that for a minute.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥やっぱり、 まれかさんの写真、か。
English: I knew it. Dessie's picture is in here.

Japanese: い、いやあ、女房おもいの ダンナよなー。
English: Yeah, I guess he really digs his wife, huh?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ち、ちがうぞ！ 成歩堂、そんなんじゃねえって！
English: Hey, Nick... It's not what you're thinking at all!

Japanese: 目的はやっぱり、まれかさんか。
English: No, I'm pretty sure it is. You haven't changed a bit.

Japanese: ‥‥いやいや！　やっぱり、 人妻はアレよな。ヤバいって！
English: Aw, no way man! I mean, she's a married woman! That's just bad news!

Japanese: （コリないヤツだな、ホント‥‥）
English: (He really IS here just to check out Ms. DeLite...)

Japanese: あの。“アルバイト先で拾った” って言ってましたよね？
English: So you said you found it at your job, right?

Japanese: ああ！　オレさ、警備会社で ガードマンやってるんだぜ！
English: Yeah! I'm working for a private security company as a guard!

Japanese: ホレちゃうだろ？　思わずさあ！
English: Chicks just love a guy in uniform, you know!

Japanese: （ガードマン‥‥。たしかに それっぽい制服を着ているな）
English: (A security guard, huh. So that's what that uniform is all about...)

Japanese: おまえがコレを拾ったの ‥‥ゆうべの何時ごろだ？
English: So what time was it when you found this last night?

Japanese: お、なんだよそれ！ いわゆるアリバイってヤツ？
English: Huh? Why are you asking that!? Trying to see if I've got an "alibi"!?

Japanese: （‥‥ゼンゼンちがうよ！）
English: (Umm... You're not the one on the hook for a crime this time, buddy.)

Japanese: たしか‥‥１時ごろかな。夜中の。 バイト先の、ビルの１階でさ。
English: I guess it was around 1:00 in the morning on the first floor of our company building.

Japanese: （どうして、優作くんのサイフが そんなところに‥‥？）
